sentences.zh: 本秦京師為內史，分天下作三十六郡。

translations.literal: Literal: The Qin capital region originally served as the Office of the Interior Historian, and the realm was divided into thirty-six commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin capital region originally served as the Office of the Interior Historian, and the realm was divided into thirty-six commanderies.

sentences.zh: 漢興，以其郡大大，稍復開置，又立諸侯王國。

translations.literal: Literal: After the Han was founded, these commanderies were gradually re-established and expanded because their territories were so large, and feudal kingdoms were also created.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han was founded, these commanderies were gradually re-established and expanded because their territories were so large, and feudal kingdoms were also created.

sentences.zh: 武帝開廣三邊。

translations.literal: Literal: Under Emperor Wu, the empire expanded on all three frontiers.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wu, the empire expanded on all three frontiers.

sentences.zh: 故自高祖增二十六，文、景各六，武帝二十八，昭帝一，訖於孝平，凡郡國一百三，縣邑千三百一十四，道三十二，侯國二百四十一。

translations.literal: Literal: Thus, from Gaozu onward, twenty-six commanderies were added; Emperor Wen and Emperor Jing each added six; Emperor Wu added twenty-eight; and Emperor Zhao added one. By Emperor Ping, there were 103 commanderies and kingdoms, 1,314 counties and cities, 32 dao circuits, and 241 marquisates.
translations.idiomatic: Thus, from Gaozu onward, twenty-six commanderies were added; Emperor Wen and Emperor Jing each added six; Emperor Wu added twenty-eight; and Emperor Zhao added one. By Emperor Ping, there were 103 commanderies and kingdoms, 1,314 counties and cities, 32 dao circuits, and 241 marquisates.

sentences.zh: 地東西九千三百二里，南北萬三千三百六十八里。

translations.literal: Literal: The realm stretched 9,302 li from east to west and 13,368 li from north to south.
translations.idiomatic: The realm stretched 9,302 li from east to west and 13,368 li from north to south.

sentences.zh: 提封田一萬萬四千五百一十三萬六千四百五頃，其一萬萬二百五十二萬八千八百八十九頃，邑居道路，山川林澤，群不可墾，其三千二百二十九萬九百四十七頃，可墾不可墾，定墾田八百二十七萬五百三十六頃。

translations.literal: Literal: Registered territory totaled 145,136,405 qing. Of this, 102,528,889 qing was occupied by settlements, roads, mountains, rivers, forests, marshes, and other uncultivable land; 32,290,947 qing was potentially arable but not all reclaimed; fixed cultivated fields amounted to 8,270,536 qing.
translations.idiomatic: Registered territory totaled 145,136,405 qing. Of this, 102,528,889 qing was occupied by settlements, roads, mountains, rivers, forests, marshes, and other uncultivable land; 32,290,947 qing was potentially arable but not all reclaimed; fixed cultivated fields amounted to 8,270,536 qing.

sentences.zh: 民戶千二百二十三萬三千六十二，口五千九百五十九萬四千九百七十八。

translations.literal: Literal: There were 12,233,062 registered households and a population of 59,594,978.
translations.idiomatic: There were 12,233,062 registered households and a population of 59,594,978.

sentences.zh: 漢極盛矣。

translations.literal: Literal: At this point, the Han had reached the height of its power.
translations.idiomatic: At this point, the Han had reached the height of its power.

sentences.zh: 凡民函五常之性，而其剛柔緩急，音聲不同，繫水土之風氣，故謂之風；

translations.literal: Literal: All people contain the five constant human dispositions, yet differences in hardness and softness, quickness and slowness, and even in speech and voice arise from the qi of local waters and soils; this is called regional character (feng).
translations.idiomatic: All people contain the five constant human dispositions, yet differences in hardness and softness, quickness and slowness, and even in speech and voice arise from the qi of local waters and soils; this is called regional character (feng).

sentences.zh: 好惡取舍，動靜亡常，隨君上之情欲，故謂之俗。

translations.literal: Literal: Preferences and aversions, choices and habits, action and repose change with the ruler’s inclinations; this is called custom (su).
translations.idiomatic: Preferences and aversions, choices and habits, action and repose change with the ruler’s inclinations; this is called custom (su).

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「移風易俗，莫善於樂。」

translations.literal: Literal: Confucius said, "Nothing is better than music for transforming regional character and changing customs."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "Nothing is better than music for transforming regional character and changing customs."

sentences.zh: 言聖王在上，統理人倫，必移其本，而易其末，此混同天下一之虖中和，然後王教成也。

translations.literal: Literal: This means that when a sage king rules above and orders human relations, he must transform the roots and then alter the branches. Only by harmonizing and unifying the realm can royal teaching be fulfilled.
translations.idiomatic: This means that when a sage king rules above and orders human relations, he must transform the roots and then alter the branches. Only by harmonizing and unifying the realm can royal teaching be fulfilled.

sentences.zh: 漢承百年之末，國土變改，民人遷徙，成帝時劉向略言其域分，丞相張禹使屬潁川朱贛條其風俗，猶未宣究，故輯而論之，終其本末著於篇。

translations.literal: Literal: At the end of Han’s first century, territories had changed and people had migrated. In Emperor Cheng’s reign, Liu Xiang sketched the regional divisions; Chancellor Zhang Yu ordered Zhu Gan of Yingchuan to record local customs. Because that effort was still incomplete, this account has been compiled and discussed here, setting out the whole from beginning to end.
translations.idiomatic: At the end of Han’s first century, territories had changed and people had migrated. In Emperor Cheng’s reign, Liu Xiang sketched the regional divisions; Chancellor Zhang Yu ordered Zhu Gan of Yingchuan to record local customs. Because that effort was still incomplete, this account has been compiled and discussed here, setting out the whole from beginning to end.

sentences.zh: 秦地，於天官東井、輿鬼之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Qin region corresponds, in celestial offices, to the sectors of Eastern Well and Carriage Ghost.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin region corresponds, in celestial offices, to the sectors of Eastern Well and Carriage Ghost.

sentences.zh: 其界自弘農故關以西，京兆、扶風、馮翊、北地、上郡、西河、安定、天水、隴西，南有巴、蜀、廣漢、犍為、武都，西有金城、武威、張掖、酒泉、敦煌，又西南有牂柯、越巂、益州，皆宜屬焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Its territory runs west from the old Hongnong Pass and includes Jingzhao, Fufeng, Fengyi, Beidi, Shang, Xihe, Anding, Tianshui, and Longxi; southward it reaches Ba, Shu, Guanghan, Jianwei, and Wudu; westward it includes Jincheng, Wuwei, Zhangye, Jiuquan, and Dunhuang; and to the southwest, Zangke, Yuexi, and Yizhou. All of these should belong under it.
translations.idiomatic: Its territory runs west from the old Hongnong Pass and includes Jingzhao, Fufeng, Fengyi, Beidi, Shang, Xihe, Anding, Tianshui, and Longxi; southward it reaches Ba, Shu, Guanghan, Jianwei, and Wudu; westward it includes Jincheng, Wuwei, Zhangye, Jiuquan, and Dunhuang; and to the southwest, Zangke, Yuexi, and Yizhou. All of these should belong under it.

sentences.zh: 秦之先曰柏益，出自帝顓頊，堯時助禹治水，為舜朕虞，養育草木鳥獸，賜姓嬴氏，歷夏、殷為諸侯。

translations.literal: Literal: Qin’s earliest ancestor was Boyi, descended from Emperor Zhuanxu. In Yao’s time he helped Yu control the floods; under Shun he served as Director of Mountains and Marshes, tending plants, trees, birds, and beasts. He was granted the surname Ying, and his line remained feudal lords through Xia and Shang.
translations.idiomatic: Qin’s earliest ancestor was Boyi, descended from Emperor Zhuanxu. In Yao’s time he helped Yu control the floods; under Shun he served as Director of Mountains and Marshes, tending plants, trees, birds, and beasts. He was granted the surname Ying, and his line remained feudal lords through Xia and Shang.

sentences.zh: 至周有造父，善馭習馬，得華騮、綠耳之乘，幸於穆王，封於趙城，故更為趙氏。

translations.literal: Literal: By Zhou times there was Zaofu, a master charioteer who trained horses and obtained the famed teams Hualiu and Luer. Favored by King Mu, he was enfeoffed at Zhaocheng, so the lineage became known as the Zhao clan.
translations.idiomatic: By Zhou times there was Zaofu, a master charioteer who trained horses and obtained the famed teams Hualiu and Luer. Favored by King Mu, he was enfeoffed at Zhaocheng, so the lineage became known as the Zhao clan.

sentences.zh: 後有非子，為周孝王養馬汧、渭之間。

translations.literal: Literal: Later came Feizi, who raised horses for King Xiao of Zhou between the Qian and Wei Rivers.
translations.idiomatic: Later came Feizi, who raised horses for King Xiao of Zhou between the Qian and Wei Rivers.

sentences.zh: 孝王曰：「昔伯益知禽獸，子孫不絕。」

translations.literal: Literal: King Xiao said, "In former times Boyi understood birds and beasts; his descendants should not be cut off."
translations.idiomatic: King Xiao said, "In former times Boyi understood birds and beasts; his descendants should not be cut off."

sentences.zh: 乃封為附庸，邑之於秦，今隴西秦亭秦谷是也。

translations.literal: Literal: So he enfeoffed him as a dependent lord and granted him a settlement at Qin, identified with Qin Pavilion and Qin Valley in present-day Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: So he enfeoffed him as a dependent lord and granted him a settlement at Qin, identified with Qin Pavilion and Qin Valley in present-day Longxi.

sentences.zh: 至玄孫，氏為莊公，破西戎，有其地。

translations.literal: Literal: By his great-grandson’s generation, the clan head was Duke Zhuang, who defeated the western Rong and took their territory.
translations.idiomatic: By his great-grandson’s generation, the clan head was Duke Zhuang, who defeated the western Rong and took their territory.

sentences.zh: 子襄公時，幽王為犬戎所敗，平王東遷雒邑。

translations.literal: Literal: In the time of Duke Xiang, King You was defeated by the Quanrong, and King Ping moved east to Luoyi.
translations.idiomatic: In the time of Duke Xiang, King You was defeated by the Quanrong, and King Ping moved east to Luoyi.

sentences.zh: 襄公將兵救周有功，賜受廄、酆之地，列為諸侯。

translations.literal: Literal: Duke Xiang led troops to rescue Zhou and was rewarded with the lands of Qiu and Feng; he was formally ranked among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Xiang led troops to rescue Zhou and was rewarded with the lands of Qiu and Feng; he was formally ranked among the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 後八世，穆公稱伯，以河為竟。

translations.literal: Literal: Eight generations later, Duke Mu became hegemon and took the Yellow River as his frontier.
translations.idiomatic: Eight generations later, Duke Mu became hegemon and took the Yellow River as his frontier.

sentences.zh: 十餘世，孝公用商君，制轅田，開仟伯，東雄諸侯。

translations.literal: Literal: More than ten generations on, Duke Xiao employed Lord Shang, reformed land institutions and opened field boundaries, and became dominant among the eastern lords.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten generations on, Duke Xiao employed Lord Shang, reformed land institutions and opened field boundaries, and became dominant among the eastern lords.

sentences.zh: 子惠公初稱王，得上郡、西河。

translations.literal: Literal: His son Duke Hui first assumed the royal title and gained Shang Commandery and Xihe.
translations.idiomatic: His son Duke Hui first assumed the royal title and gained Shang Commandery and Xihe.

sentences.zh: 孫昭王開巴蜀，滅周，取九鼎。

translations.literal: Literal: His grandson King Zhao opened Ba and Shu, destroyed Zhou, and seized the Nine Tripods.
translations.idiomatic: His grandson King Zhao opened Ba and Shu, destroyed Zhou, and seized the Nine Tripods.

sentences.zh: 昭王曾孫政并六國，稱皇帝，負力怙威，燔書阬儒，自任私智。

translations.literal: Literal: King Zhao’s great-grandson Zheng annexed the six states and proclaimed himself emperor. Relying on force and power, he burned books, buried Ru scholars, and trusted only his private judgment.
translations.idiomatic: King Zhao’s great-grandson Zheng annexed the six states and proclaimed himself emperor. Relying on force and power, he burned books, buried Ru scholars, and trusted only his private judgment.

sentences.zh: 至子胡亥，天下畔之。

translations.literal: Literal: By the time of his son Hu Hai, the whole realm had turned against Qin.
translations.idiomatic: By the time of his son Hu Hai, the whole realm had turned against Qin.

sentences.zh: 天水、隴西，山多林木，民以板為室屋。

translations.literal: Literal: In Tianshui and Longxi, mountains and forests are abundant, and people build houses of timber planks.
translations.idiomatic: In Tianshui and Longxi, mountains and forests are abundant, and people build houses of timber planks.

sentences.zh: 及安定、北地、上郡、西河，皆迫近戎狄，修習戰備，高上氣力，以射獵為先。

translations.literal: Literal: Anding, Beidi, Shang, and Xihe all bordered Rong and Di peoples, so they cultivated military readiness, prized physical strength, and treated archery and hunting as primary skills.
translations.idiomatic: Anding, Beidi, Shang, and Xihe all bordered Rong and Di peoples, so they cultivated military readiness, prized physical strength, and treated archery and hunting as primary skills.

sentences.zh: 故《秦詩》曰「在其板屋」；

translations.literal: Literal: Hence the Qin Odes say, "They dwell in their plank houses."
translations.idiomatic: Hence the Qin Odes say, "They dwell in their plank houses."

sentences.zh: 又曰「王于興師，修我甲兵，與子偕行」。

translations.literal: Literal: And again: "The king is raising troops; repair my armor and weapons, and march with me."
translations.idiomatic: And again: "The king is raising troops; repair my armor and weapons, and march with me."

sentences.zh: 及車轔、四臷、小戎之篇，皆言車馬田狩之事。

translations.literal: Literal: The poems "Chelin," "Si Tie," and "Xiao Rong" likewise all speak of chariots, horses, and the hunt.
translations.idiomatic: The poems "Chelin," "Si Tie," and "Xiao Rong" likewise all speak of chariots, horses, and the hunt.

sentences.zh: 漢興，六郡良家子選給羽林、期門，以材力為官，名將多出焉。

translations.literal: Literal: After Han arose, sons of good families from the six commanderies were selected for the Yulin and Qimen guards, and office was granted by strength and ability; many noted generals came from there.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han was founded, sons of good families from these six commanderies were selected into the Yulin and Qimen imperial guards, with appointment based on physical ability; many famous generals emerged from them.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「君子有勇而亡誼則為亂，小人有勇而亡誼則為盜。」

translations.literal: Literal: Confucius said, "If a gentleman has courage but no righteousness, he causes disorder; if a petty man has courage but no righteousness, he becomes a thief."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "If a gentleman has courage but no righteousness, he causes disorder; if a petty man has courage but no righteousness, he becomes a thief."

sentences.zh: 故此數郡，民俗質木，不恥寇盜。

translations.literal: Literal: So in these commanderies, popular habits were plain and rough, and people did not regard banditry as shameful.
translations.idiomatic: So in these commanderies, popular habits were plain and rough, and people did not regard banditry as shameful.

sentences.zh: 故秦地於禹貢時跨雍、梁二州，詩風兼秦、豳兩國。

translations.literal: Literal: Thus in the Yu Gong era, Qin territory straddled Yong and Liang provinces, and in the Odes its regional airs overlapped those of Qin and Bin alike.
translations.idiomatic: Thus in the Yu Gong era, Qin territory straddled Yong and Liang provinces, and in the Odes its regional airs overlapped those of Qin and Bin alike.

sentences.zh: 昔后稷封鹅，公劉處豳，大王徙廄，文王作酆，武王治鎬，其民有先王遺風，好稼穡，務本業，故豳詩言農桑衣食之本甚備。

translations.literal: Literal: In antiquity Houji was enfeoffed, Gongliu settled in Bin, the Great King moved to Qiu, King Wen established Feng, and King Wu governed Hao. Their people retained the legacy of former kings: they valued farming and sericulture and honored basic livelihoods, which is why the Bin Odes treat the foundations of food and clothing in such detail.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity Houji was enfeoffed, Gongliu settled in Bin, the Great King moved to Qiu, King Wen established Feng, and King Wu governed Hao. Their people retained the legacy of former kings: they valued farming and sericulture and honored basic livelihoods, which is why the Bin Odes treat the foundations of food and clothing in such detail.

sentences.zh: 有鄠、杜竹林，南山檀柘，號稱陸海，為九州膏腴。

translations.literal: Literal: There were bamboo groves at Hu and Du, and stands of sandalwood and zhe in the southern mountains. The region was called a "land sea" and counted among the richest soils in the Nine Provinces.
translations.idiomatic: There were bamboo groves at Hu and Du, and stands of sandalwood and zhe in the southern mountains. The region was called a "land sea" and counted among the richest soils in the Nine Provinces.

sentences.zh: 始皇之初，鄭國穿渠，引涇水溉田，沃野千里，民以富饒。

translations.literal: Literal: Early in the First Emperor’s reign, Zheng Guo cut the canal and drew Jing River water to irrigate fields; fertile plains stretched for a thousand li, and the people became prosperous.
translations.idiomatic: Early in the First Emperor’s reign, Zheng Guo cut the canal and drew Jing River water to irrigate fields; fertile plains stretched for a thousand li, and the people became prosperous.

sentences.zh: 漢興，立都長安，徙齊諸田，楚昭、屈、景及諸功臣家於長陵。

translations.literal: Literal: After Han arose and made Chang’an the capital, it relocated the Tian clans of Qi, the Zhao, Qu, and Jing lineages of Chu, and the households of meritorious officials to Changling.
translations.idiomatic: After Han arose and made Chang’an the capital, it relocated the Tian clans of Qi, the Zhao, Qu, and Jing lineages of Chu, and the households of meritorious officials to Changling.

sentences.zh: 後世世徙吏二千石、高訾富人及豪桀并兼之家於諸陵。

translations.literal: Literal: In later times, the court repeatedly relocated two-thousand-shi officials, very wealthy households, and powerful landholding families to settlements around the imperial mausoleums.
translations.idiomatic: In later times, the court repeatedly relocated two-thousand-shi officials, very wealthy households, and powerful landholding families to settlements around the imperial mausoleums.

sentences.zh: 蓋亦以彊幹弱支，非獨為奉山園也。

translations.literal: Literal: This was also a policy of strengthening the trunk and weakening the branches, not merely a matter of servicing the imperial parks and tomb precincts.
translations.idiomatic: This was also a policy of strengthening the trunk and weakening the branches, not merely a matter of servicing the imperial parks and tomb precincts.

sentences.zh: 是故五方雜厝，風俗不純。

translations.literal: Literal: As a result, people from all directions were mixed together, and local customs became impure.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, people from all directions were mixed together, and local customs became impure.

sentences.zh: 其世家則好禮文，富人則商賈為利，豪桀則游俠通姦。

translations.literal: Literal: Great hereditary families favored rites and letters, the rich pursued profit through trade, and local strongmen cultivated knight-errant networks and illicit associations.
translations.idiomatic: Great hereditary families favored rites and letters, the rich pursued profit through trade, and local strongmen cultivated knight-errant networks and illicit associations.

sentences.zh: 瀕南山，近夏陽，多阻險輕薄，易為盜賊，常為天下劇。

translations.literal: Literal: Along the southern mountains and near Xiayang, the terrain was difficult and people could be rash and unruly, making banditry easy; these districts were often among the most troublesome in the empire.
translations.idiomatic: Along the southern mountains and near Xiayang, the terrain was difficult and people could be rash and unruly, making banditry easy; these districts were often among the most troublesome in the empire.

sentences.zh: 又郡國輻湊，浮食者多，民去本就末，列侯貴人車服僭上，眾庶放效，羞不相及，嫁娶尤崇侈靡，送死過度。

translations.literal: Literal: Because commanderies and kingdoms converged there, many lived by floating, non-productive livelihoods. People abandoned basic occupations for secondary ones; marquises and nobles used carriages and dress beyond their rank; commoners copied them and competed in status. Weddings became extravagantly lavish, and funerary expenses were excessive.
translations.idiomatic: Because commanderies and kingdoms converged there, many lived by floating, non-productive livelihoods. People abandoned basic occupations for secondary ones; marquises and nobles used carriages and dress beyond their rank; commoners copied them and competed in status. Weddings became extravagantly lavish, and funerary expenses were excessive.

sentences.zh: 自武威以西，本匈奴昆邪王、休屠王地，武帝時攘之，初置四郡，以通西域，鬲絕南羌、匈奴。

translations.literal: Literal: West of Wuwei had originally been the lands of the Xiongnu kings Kunye and Xiutu. Emperor Wu seized them and first established four commanderies there to open routes to the Western Regions and to split apart the Southern Qiang and Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: West of Wuwei had originally been the lands of the Xiongnu kings Kunye and Xiutu. Emperor Wu seized them and first established four commanderies there to open routes to the Western Regions and to split apart the Southern Qiang and Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 其民或以關東下貧，或以報怨過當，或以誖逆亡道，家屬徙焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Some inhabitants were moved there because they were poor people from east of the passes, some because they had carried revenge too far, and some because they had rebelled or become outlaws; their families were resettled there as well.
translations.idiomatic: Some inhabitants were moved there because they were poor people from east of the passes, some because they had carried revenge too far, and some because they had rebelled or become outlaws; their families were resettled there as well.

sentences.zh: 習俗頗殊，地廣民稀，水屮宜畜牧，古涼州之畜為天下饒。

translations.literal: Literal: Local habits differed markedly. The land was broad and population sparse; water and grass favored herding, and livestock from old Liangzhou were among the richest in the empire.
translations.idiomatic: Local habits differed markedly. The land was broad and population sparse; water and grass favored herding, and livestock from old Liangzhou were among the richest in the empire.

sentences.zh: 保邊塞，二千石治之，咸以兵馬為務；

translations.literal: Literal: To secure the frontier defenses, two-thousand-shi administrators governed these places, all treating military and cavalry affairs as their main duty.
translations.idiomatic: To secure the frontier defenses, two-thousand-shi administrators governed these places, all treating military and cavalry affairs as their main duty.

sentences.zh: 酒禮之會，上下通焉，吏民相親。

translations.literal: Literal: At wine and ritual gatherings, superiors and subordinates mingled; officials and commoners were close to one another.
translations.idiomatic: At wine and ritual gatherings, superiors and subordinates mingled; officials and commoners were close to one another.

sentences.zh: 是以其俗風雨時節，穀糴常賤，少盜賊，有和氣之應，賢於內郡。

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore their climate was seasonable, grain prices were usually low, theft was rare, and social harmony was evident; in these respects they were better than the interior commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore their climate was seasonable, grain prices were usually low, theft was rare, and social harmony was evident; in these respects they were better than the interior commanderies.

sentences.zh: 此政寬厚，吏不苛刻之所致也。

translations.literal: Literal: This came from broad and generous governance and from officials who were not harsh.
translations.idiomatic: This came from broad and generous governance and from officials who were not harsh.

sentences.zh: 巴、蜀、廣漢本南夷，秦并以為郡，土地肥美，有江水沃野，山林竹木疏食果實之饒。

translations.literal: Literal: Ba, Shu, and Guanghan had originally been southern non-Han regions. Qin annexed them as commanderies. Their lands were rich and fertile, with river-watered plains and abundant mountains, forests, bamboo, timber, edible plants, and fruit.
translations.idiomatic: Ba, Shu, and Guanghan had originally been southern non-Han regions. Qin annexed them as commanderies. Their lands were rich and fertile, with river-watered plains and abundant mountains, forests, bamboo, timber, edible plants, and fruit.

sentences.zh: 南賈滇、僰僮，西近邛、莋馬旄牛。

translations.literal: Literal: To the south, merchants traded with Dian, Bo, and Tong peoples; to the west they bordered Qiong and Zuo regions known for horses and long-haired cattle.
translations.idiomatic: To the south, merchants traded with Dian, Bo, and Tong peoples; to the west they bordered Qiong and Zuo regions known for horses and long-haired cattle.

sentences.zh: 民食稻魚，亡凶年憂，俗不愁苦，而輕易淫泆，柔弱褊阨。

translations.literal: Literal: People lived on rice and fish and did not fear famine years. Customs were generally free from hardship, but also tended toward laxity and indulgence, with a soft and somewhat constricted social temperament.
translations.idiomatic: People lived on rice and fish and did not fear famine years. Customs were generally free from hardship, but also tended toward laxity and indulgence, with a soft and somewhat constricted social temperament.

sentences.zh: 景、武間，文翁為蜀守，教民讀書法令，未能篤信道德，反以好文刺譏，貴慕權勢。

translations.literal: Literal: Between the Jing and Wu eras, Wen Weng governed Shu, teaching people to read and to know the laws. Yet they did not become deeply grounded in moral doctrine; instead they came to prize clever literary criticism and to admire power and influence.
translations.idiomatic: Between the Jing and Wu eras, Wen Weng governed Shu, teaching people to read and to know the laws. Yet they did not become deeply grounded in moral doctrine; instead they came to prize clever literary criticism and to admire power and influence.

sentences.zh: 及司馬相如游宦京師諸侯，以文辭顯於世，鄉黨慕循其跡。

translations.literal: Literal: When Sima Xiangru served in the capital and among feudal courts and became famous for his writings, people of his home region eagerly imitated his path.
translations.idiomatic: When Sima Xiangru served in the capital and among feudal courts and became famous for his writings, people of his home region eagerly imitated his path.

sentences.zh: 後有王褒、嚴遵、揚雄之徒，文章冠天下。

translations.literal: Literal: Later came figures such as Wang Bao, Yan Zun, and Yang Xiong, whose writing ranked at the top in the empire.
translations.idiomatic: Later came figures such as Wang Bao, Yan Zun, and Yang Xiong, whose writing ranked at the top in the empire.

sentences.zh: 繇文翁倡其教，相如為之師，故孔子曰：「有教亡類。」

translations.literal: Literal: Because Wen Weng had first promoted education and Xiangru then became its model, this confirms Confucius’s saying: "In teaching, there should be no class distinctions."
translations.idiomatic: Because Wen Weng had first promoted education and Xiangru then became its model, this confirms Confucius’s saying: "In teaching, there should be no class distinctions."

sentences.zh: 武都地雜氐、羌，及犍為、牂柯、越巂，皆西南外夷，武帝初開置。

translations.literal: Literal: Wudu was ethnically mixed with Di and Qiang peoples; Jianwei, Zangke, and Yuexi were all southwestern frontier regions first opened and established in Emperor Wu’s reign.
translations.idiomatic: Wudu was ethnically mixed with Di and Qiang peoples; Jianwei, Zangke, and Yuexi were all southwestern frontier regions first opened and established in Emperor Wu’s reign.

sentences.zh: 民俗略與巴、蜀同，而武都近天水，俗頗似焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Their customs were broadly similar to Ba and Shu; and because Wudu lay near Tianshui, some of its habits resembled Tianshui as well.
translations.idiomatic: Their customs were broadly similar to Ba and Shu; and because Wudu lay near Tianshui, some of its habits resembled Tianshui as well.

sentences.zh: 故秦地天下三分之一，而人眾不過什三，然量其富居什六。

translations.literal: Literal: Thus Qin lands made up one third of the realm, while its population was no more than three-tenths; yet in assessed wealth it held roughly six-tenths.
translations.idiomatic: Thus Qin lands made up one third of the realm, while its population was no more than three-tenths; yet in assessed wealth it held roughly six-tenths.

sentences.zh: 秦豳吳札觀樂，為之歌秦，曰：「此之謂夏聲。

translations.literal: Literal: When Wu Zha observed court music in Qin and Bin, the Qin piece was sung as: "This is what is called the sound of Xia.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Zha observed court music in Qin and Bin, the Qin piece was sung as: "This is what is called the sound of Xia.

sentences.zh: 夫能夏則大，大之至也，其周舊乎？」

translations.literal: Literal: To be able to embody Xia is to be great; this is greatness at its fullest. Does it preserve something of old Zhou?"
translations.idiomatic: To be able to embody Xia is to be great; this is greatness at its fullest. Does it preserve something of old Zhou?"

sentences.zh: 自井十度至柳三度，謂之鶉首之次，秦之分也。

translations.literal: Literal: From the 10th degree of Well to the 3rd degree of Willow is called the Quail-Head station; this is Qin’s celestial division.
translations.idiomatic: From the 10th degree of Well to the 3rd degree of Willow is called the Quail-Head station; this is Qin’s celestial division.

sentences.zh: 魏地，觜觿、參之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Wei region corresponds to the stellar fields of Zixi and Shen.
translations.idiomatic: The Wei region corresponds to the stellar fields of Zixi and Shen.

sentences.zh: 其界自高陵以東，盡河東、河內，南有陳留及汝南之召陵、郦彊、新汲、西華、長平，潁川之舞陽、郾、許、傿陵，河南之開封、中牟、陽武、酸棗、卷，皆魏分也。

translations.literal: Literal: Its borders run east from Gaoling, including all of Hedong and Henei; to the south it includes Chenliu and, in Runan, Shaoling, Liqiang, Xinji, Xihua, and Changping; in Yingchuan, Wuyang, Yan, Xu, and Yanling; and in Henan, Kaifeng, Zhongmu, Yangwu, Suanzao, and Juan. All these belong to Wei’s division.
translations.idiomatic: Its borders run east from Gaoling, including all of Hedong and Henei; to the south it includes Chenliu and, in Runan, Shaoling, Liqiang, Xinji, Xihua, and Changping; in Yingchuan, Wuyang, Yan, Xu, and Yanling; and in Henan, Kaifeng, Zhongmu, Yangwu, Suanzao, and Juan. All these belong to Wei’s division.

sentences.zh: 河內本殷之舊都，周既滅殷，分其畿內為三國，詩風邶、庸、衛國是也。

translations.literal: Literal: Henei had originally been the old Shang capital. After Zhou destroyed Shang, it split the royal domain there into three states: Bei, Yong, and Wei, as reflected in the Odes.
translations.idiomatic: Henei had originally been the old Shang capital. After Zhou destroyed Shang, it split the royal domain there into three states: Bei, Yong, and Wei, as reflected in the Odes.

sentences.zh: 鄁，以封紂子武庚；

translations.literal: Literal: Bei was granted to Wu Geng, son of King Zhou of Shang;
translations.idiomatic: Bei was granted to Wu Geng, son of King Zhou of Shang;.

sentences.zh: 庸，管叔尹之；

translations.literal: Literal: Yong was entrusted to Guan Shu;
translations.idiomatic: Yong was entrusted to Guan Shu;.

sentences.zh: 衛，蔡叔尹之：以監殷民，謂之三監。

translations.literal: Literal: and Wei to Cai Shu, all to supervise the Shang people; these were called the Three Supervisors.
translations.idiomatic: And Wei to Cai Shu, all to supervise the Shang people; these were called the Three Supervisors.

sentences.zh: 故《書序》曰「武王崩，三監畔」，周公誅之，盡以其地封弟康叔，號曰孟侯，以夾輔周室；

translations.literal: Literal: Hence the preface to the Documents says, "When King Wu died, the Three Supervisors rebelled." The Duke of Zhou executed them and enfeoffed all their lands to his younger brother Kang Shu, styled Menghou, to support the Zhou royal house.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the preface to the Documents says, "When King Wu died, the Three Supervisors rebelled." The Duke of Zhou executed them and enfeoffed all their lands to his younger brother Kang Shu, styled Menghou, to support the Zhou royal house.

sentences.zh: 遷邶、庸之民于雒邑，故邶、庸、衛三國之詩相與同風。

translations.literal: Literal: He also moved the peoples of Bei and Yong to Luoyi, which is why the Odes of Bei, Yong, and Wei share the same regional air.
translations.idiomatic: He also moved the peoples of Bei and Yong to Luoyi, which is why the Odes of Bei, Yong, and Wei share the same regional air.

sentences.zh: 《邶詩》曰「在浚之下」，《庸》曰「在浚之郊」；

translations.literal: Literal: The Bei Odes say, "Below Jun"; the Yong Odes say, "In the outskirts of Jun."
translations.idiomatic: The Bei Odes say, "Below Jun"; the Yong Odes say, "In the outskirts of Jun."

sentences.zh: 《邶》又曰「亦流于淇」，「河水洋洋」，《庸》曰「送我淇上」，「在彼中河」，《衛》曰「瞻彼淇奧」，「河水洋洋」。

translations.literal: Literal: Bei also says, "Flowing into the Qi," and "The river waters surge"; Yong says, "Seeing me off above the Qi," and "There in the middle of the river"; Wei says, "Look at the bends of the Qi," and again, "The river waters surge."
translations.idiomatic: Bei also says, "Flowing into the Qi," and "The river waters surge"; Yong says, "Seeing me off above the Qi," and "There in the middle of the river"; Wei says, "Look at the bends of the Qi," and again, "The river waters surge."

sentences.zh: 故吳公子札聘魯觀周樂，聞邶、庸、衛之歌，曰：「美哉淵乎！

translations.literal: Literal: So when Prince Zha of Wu visited Lu and heard the songs of Bei, Yong, and Wei in the Zhou music, he said, "How beautiful and deep!
translations.idiomatic: So when Prince Zha of Wu visited Lu and heard the songs of Bei, Yong, and Wei in the Zhou music, he said, "How beautiful and deep!

sentences.zh: 吾聞康叔之德如是，是其衛風乎？」

translations.literal: Literal: I have heard that this was Kang Shu’s virtue; is this the Airs of Wei?"
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that this was Kang Shu’s virtue; is this the Airs of Wei?"

sentences.zh: 至十六世，懿公亡道，為狄所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: By the sixteenth generation, Duke Yi had lost the Way and was destroyed by the Di.
translations.idiomatic: By the sixteenth generation, Duke Yi had lost the Way and was destroyed by the Di.

sentences.zh: 齊桓公帥諸侯伐狄，而更封衛於河南曹、楚丘，是為文公。

translations.literal: Literal: Duke Huan of Qi led the feudal lords against the Di and re-enfeoffed Wei at Cao and Chuqiu in Henan; this ruler was Duke Wen of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan of Qi led the feudal lords against the Di and re-enfeoffed Wei at Cao and Chuqiu in Henan; this ruler was Duke Wen of Wei.

sentences.zh: 而河內殷虛，更屬于晉。

translations.literal: Literal: The old Shang ruins in Henei were then attached to Jin.
translations.idiomatic: The old Shang ruins in Henei were then attached to Jin.

sentences.zh: 康叔之風既歇，而紂之化猶存，故俗剛彊，多豪桀侵奪，薄恩禮，好生分。

translations.literal: Literal: By then Kang Shu’s influence had faded, while the legacy of King Zhou of Shang still remained; thus local habits were hard and aggressive, with many strongmen given to seizure and encroachment, slighting benevolence and propriety and loving factional division.
translations.idiomatic: By then Kang Shu’s influence had faded, while the legacy of King Zhou of Shang still remained; thus local habits were hard and aggressive, with many strongmen given to seizure and encroachment, slighting benevolence and propriety and loving factional division.

sentences.zh: 河東土地平易，有鹽鐵之饒，本唐堯所居，詩風唐、魏之國也。

translations.literal: Literal: Hedong had flat and accessible land and rich resources of salt and iron. It was originally where Tang Yao had lived, and in Odes terms belonged to the airs of Tang and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Hedong had flat and accessible land and rich resources of salt and iron. It was originally where Tang Yao had lived, and in Odes terms belonged to the airs of Tang and Wei.

sentences.zh: 周武王子唐叔在母未生，武王夢帝謂己曰：「余名而子曰虞，將與之唐，屬之參。」

translations.literal: Literal: When Tang Shu, son of King Wu of Zhou, was still in his mother’s womb, King Wu dreamed that the High God told him: "Name your son Yu. I will give him Tang and assign him to Shen."
translations.idiomatic: When Tang Shu, son of King Wu of Zhou, was still in his mother’s womb, King Wu dreamed that the High God told him: "Name your son Yu. I will give him Tang and assign him to Shen."

sentences.zh: 及生，名之曰虞。

translations.literal: Literal: When he was born, he was named Yu.
translations.idiomatic: When he was born, he was named Yu.

sentences.zh: 至成王滅唐，而封叔虞。

translations.literal: Literal: In King Cheng’s reign, Tang was extinguished and Shu Yu was enfeoffed there.
translations.idiomatic: In King Cheng’s reign, Tang was extinguished and Shu Yu was enfeoffed there.

sentences.zh: 唐有晉水，及叔虞子燮為晉侯云，故參為晉星。

translations.literal: Literal: Tang had the Jin River; and because Shu Yu’s son Xie became Marquis of Jin, Shen became the stellar sign of Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Tang had the Jin River; and because Shu Yu’s son Xie became Marquis of Jin, Shen became the stellar sign of Jin.

sentences.zh: 其民有先王遺教，君子深思，小人儉陋。

translations.literal: Literal: Its people retained teachings from former kings: gentlemen were reflective, while commoners were frugal and plain.
translations.idiomatic: Its people retained teachings from former kings: gentlemen were reflective, while commoners were frugal and plain.

sentences.zh: 故唐詩蟋蟀、山樞、葛生之篇曰「今我不樂，日月其邁」；

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore poems such as "Crickets," "Mountain Pivot," and "Ge Sheng" in the Tang Odes say, "If I do not take joy now, the days and months will pass";
translations.idiomatic: Therefore poems such as "Crickets," "Mountain Pivot," and "Ge Sheng" in the Tang Odes say, "If I do not take joy now, the days and months will pass";.

sentences.zh: 「宛其死矣，它人是媮」；

translations.literal: Literal: "Once we are dead and gone, others will enjoy what was ours";
translations.idiomatic: "Once we are dead and gone, others will enjoy what was ours";.

sentences.zh: 「百歲之後，歸于其居」。

translations.literal: Literal: and "After a hundred years, we return to our dwelling."
translations.idiomatic: And "After a hundred years, we return to our dwelling."

sentences.zh: 皆思奢儉之中，念死生之慮。

translations.literal: Literal: All express reflection on balancing luxury and thrift and on anxieties about life and death.
translations.idiomatic: All express reflection on balancing luxury and thrift and on anxieties about life and death.

sentences.zh: 吳札聞唐之歌，曰：「思深哉！

translations.literal: Literal: When Wu Zha heard the songs of Tang, he said, "How profound this reflection is!
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Zha heard the songs of Tang, he said, "How profound this reflection is!

sentences.zh: 其有陶唐氏之遺民乎？」

translations.literal: Literal: Could these be descendants of Tao Tang?"
translations.idiomatic: Could these be descendants of Tao Tang?"

sentences.zh: 魏國，亦姬姓也，在晉之南河曲，故其《詩》曰「彼汾一曲」；

translations.literal: Literal: The state of Wei was also of Ji surname and lay on the southern bend of the river in Jin, so its Odes say, "That bend in the Fen";
translations.idiomatic: The state of Wei was also of Ji surname and lay on the southern bend of the river in Jin, so its Odes say, "That bend in the Fen";.

sentences.zh: 「寘諸河之側」。

translations.literal: Literal: and "Set it by the riverbank."
translations.idiomatic: And "Set it by the riverbank."

sentences.zh: 自唐叔十六世至獻公，滅魏以封大夫畢萬，滅耿以封大夫趙夙，及大夫韓武子食采於韓原，晉於是始大。

translations.literal: Literal: From Tang Shu to Duke Xian, sixteen generations passed. He destroyed Wei and enfeoffed the officer Bi Wan there, destroyed Geng and enfeoffed Zhao Su there, and granted Han Yuan as a fief to the officer Han Wuzi; from this point Jin first became powerful.
translations.idiomatic: From Tang Shu to Duke Xian, sixteen generations passed. He destroyed Wei and enfeoffed the officer Bi Wan there, destroyed Geng and enfeoffed Zhao Su there, and granted Han Yuan as a fief to the officer Han Wuzi; from this point Jin first became powerful.

sentences.zh: 至於文公，伯諸侯，尊周室，始有河內之土。

translations.literal: Literal: By Duke Wen’s time, Jin had become hegemon of the feudal lords, honored the Zhou royal house, and first acquired Henei territory.
translations.idiomatic: By Duke Wen’s time, Jin had become hegemon of the feudal lords, honored the Zhou royal house, and first acquired Henei territory.

sentences.zh: 吳札聞魏之歌，曰：「美哉渢渢乎！

translations.literal: Literal: When Wu Zha heard the songs of Wei, he said, "How fine and stirring they are!
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Zha heard the songs of Wei, he said, "How fine and stirring they are!

sentences.zh: 以德輔此，則明主也。」

translations.literal: Literal: If virtue supports this, then one has an enlightened ruler."
translations.idiomatic: If virtue supports this, then one has an enlightened ruler."

sentences.zh: 文公後十六世為韓、魏、趙所滅，三家皆自立為諸侯，是為三晉。

translations.literal: Literal: Sixteen generations after Duke Wen, Jin was destroyed by Han, Wei, and Zhao; the three houses each set themselves up as feudal lords, known as the Three Jins.
translations.idiomatic: Sixteen generations after Duke Wen, Jin was destroyed by Han, Wei, and Zhao; the three houses each set themselves up as feudal lords, known as the Three Jins.

sentences.zh: 趙與秦同祖，韓、趙皆姬姓也。

translations.literal: Literal: Zhao shared common ancestry with Qin, and both Han and Zhao were of the Ji surname.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao shared common ancestry with Qin, and both Han and Zhao were of the Ji surname.

sentences.zh: 自畢萬後十世稱侯，至孫稱王，徙都大梁，故魏一號為梁，七世為秦所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: Ten generations after Bi Wan, the line took the marquis title; by a grandson’s time it called itself king and moved the capital to Daliang. Hence Wei was also called Liang; after seven generations it was destroyed by Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Ten generations after Bi Wan, the line took the marquis title; by a grandson’s time it called itself king and moved the capital to Daliang. Hence Wei was also called Liang; after seven generations it was destroyed by Qin.

sentences.zh: 周地，柳、七星、張之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zhou region corresponds to the stellar fields of Willow, Seven Stars, and Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou region corresponds to the stellar fields of Willow, Seven Stars, and Zhang.

sentences.zh: 今之河南雒陽、穀成、平陰、偃師、鞏、緱氏，是其分也。

translations.literal: Literal: In present terms, Luoyang, Gucheng, Pingyin, Yanshi, Gong, and Goushi in Henan all belong to this division.
translations.idiomatic: In present terms, Luoyang, Gucheng, Pingyin, Yanshi, Gong, and Goushi in Henan all belong to this division.

sentences.zh: 昔周公營雒邑，以為在于土中，諸侯蕃屏四方，故立京師。

translations.literal: Literal: In former times the Duke of Zhou planned Luoyi as the center of the realm, with feudal states screening the four directions, and therefore established it as a capital.
translations.idiomatic: In former times the Duke of Zhou planned Luoyi as the center of the realm, with feudal states screening the four directions, and therefore established it as a capital.

sentences.zh: 至幽王淫褒姒，以滅宗周，子平王東居雒邑。

translations.literal: Literal: By King You’s time, indulgence with Bao Si led to the destruction of Western Zhou; his son King Ping moved east and resided at Luoyi.
translations.idiomatic: By King You’s time, indulgence with Bao Si led to the destruction of Western Zhou; his son King Ping moved east and resided at Luoyi.

sentences.zh: 其後五伯更帥諸侯以尊周室，故周於三代最為長久。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward, the Five Hegemons successively led the feudal lords in honoring the Zhou house, which is why Zhou lasted longest among the three dynasties.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, the Five Hegemons successively led the feudal lords in honoring the Zhou house, which is why Zhou lasted longest among the three dynasties.

sentences.zh: 八百餘年至於赧王，乃為秦所兼。

translations.literal: Literal: After more than eight hundred years, down to King Nan, it was finally annexed by Qin.
translations.idiomatic: After more than eight hundred years, down to King Nan, it was finally annexed by Qin.

sentences.zh: 初雒邑與宗周通封畿，東西長而南北短，短長相覆為千里。

translations.literal: Literal: Originally Luoyi and Zongzhou shared a connected royal domain, long east-west and short north-south; the overlapping dimensions totaled about a thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: Originally Luoyi and Zongzhou shared a connected royal domain, long east-west and short north-south; the overlapping dimensions totaled about a thousand li.

sentences.zh: 至襄王以河內賜晉文公，又為諸侯所侵，故其分墬小。

translations.literal: Literal: When King Xiang granted Henei to Duke Wen of Jin, and further losses followed through encroachment by other lords, Zhou’s territorial share became small.
translations.idiomatic: When King Xiang granted Henei to Duke Wen of Jin, and further losses followed through encroachment by other lords, Zhou’s territorial share became small.

sentences.zh: 周人之失，巧偽趨利，貴財賤義，高富下貧，憙為商賈，不好仕宦。

translations.literal: Literal: The failings of the Zhou people were clever deceit and profit-seeking: they valued wealth over righteousness, honored the rich and slighted the poor, delighted in commerce, and did not favor official service.
translations.idiomatic: The failings of the Zhou people were clever deceit and profit-seeking: they valued wealth over righteousness, honored the rich and slighted the poor, delighted in commerce, and did not favor official service.

sentences.zh: 自柳三度至張十二度，謂之鶉火之次，周之分也。

translations.literal: Literal: From the 3rd degree of Willow to the 12th degree of Zhang is called the Quail-Fire station; this is Zhou’s division.
translations.idiomatic: From the 3rd degree of Willow to the 12th degree of Zhang is called the Quail-Fire station; this is Zhou’s division.

sentences.zh: 韓地，角、亢、氐之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Han region corresponds to the stellar fields of Jiao, Kang, and Di.
translations.idiomatic: The Han region corresponds to the stellar fields of Jiao, Kang, and Di.

sentences.zh: 韓分晉得南陽郡及潁川之父城、定陵、襄城、潁陽、潁陰、長社、陽翟、郟，東接汝南，西接弘農得新安、宜陽，皆韓分也。

translations.literal: Literal: Han took from Jin Nanyang Commandery and, in Yingchuan, Fucheng, Dingling, Xiangcheng, Yingyang, Yingyin, Changshe, Yangdi, and Jia; it bordered Runan to the east and, to the west, reached Hongnong with Xin’an and Yiyang. All these belong to Han’s division.
translations.idiomatic: Han took from Jin Nanyang Commandery and, in Yingchuan, Fucheng, Dingling, Xiangcheng, Yingyang, Yingyin, Changshe, Yangdi, and Jia; it bordered Runan to the east and, to the west, reached Hongnong with Xin’an and Yiyang. All these belong to Han’s division.

sentences.zh: 及詩風陳、鄭之國，與韓同星分焉。

translations.literal: Literal: The Odes airs of Chen and Zheng also share the same stellar division as Han.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes airs of Chen and Zheng also share the same stellar division as Han.

sentences.zh: 陳國，今淮陽之地。

translations.literal: Literal: The state of Chen corresponds to present-day Huaiyang.
translations.idiomatic: The state of Chen corresponds to present-day Huaiyang.

sentences.zh: 陳本太昊之虛，周武王封舜後媯滿於陳，是為胡公，妻以元女大姬。

translations.literal: Literal: Chen had originally been the old seat of Taihao. King Wu of Zhou enfeoffed Gui Man, descendant of Shun, at Chen; this was Duke Hu, who married the eldest royal daughter, Da Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Chen had originally been the old seat of Taihao. King Wu of Zhou enfeoffed Gui Man, descendant of Shun, at Chen; this was Duke Hu, who married the eldest royal daughter, Da Ji.

sentences.zh: 婦人尊貴，好祭祀，用史巫，故其俗巫鬼。

translations.literal: Literal: Women there held honored status, and people favored sacrifices and relied on scribes and shamans; thus local custom was strongly devoted to spirit-medium practices.
translations.idiomatic: Women there held honored status, and people favored sacrifices and relied on scribes and shamans; thus local custom was strongly devoted to spirit-medium practices.

sentences.zh: 《陳詩》曰：「坎其擊鼓，宛丘之下，亡冬亡夏，值其鷺羽。」

translations.literal: Literal: The Chen Odes says: "How they beat the drum in rhythm below Wanqu; winter or summer without rest, waving those egret plumes."
translations.idiomatic: The Chen Odes says: "How they beat the drum in rhythm below Wanqu; winter or summer without rest, waving those egret plumes."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「東門之枌，宛丘之栩，子仲之子，婆娑其下。」

translations.literal: Literal: Another passage says: "By the ash trees at the east gate, by the oaks of Wanqu, the daughter of Zizhong dances in swaying steps beneath them."
translations.idiomatic: Another passage says: "By the ash trees at the east gate, by the oaks of Wanqu, the daughter of Zizhong dances in swaying steps beneath them."

sentences.zh: 此其風也。

translations.literal: Literal: That was the character of its regional style.
translations.idiomatic: That was the character of its regional style.

sentences.zh: 吳札聞陳之歌，曰：「國亡主，其能久乎！」

translations.literal: Literal: When Wu Zha heard the songs of Chen, he said, "A state without a ruler cannot last long."
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Zha heard the songs of Chen, he said, "A state without a ruler cannot last long."

sentences.zh: 自胡公後二十三世為楚所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: Twenty-three generations after Duke Hu, Chen was destroyed by Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Twenty-three generations after Duke Hu, Chen was destroyed by Chu.

sentences.zh: 陳雖屬楚，於天文自若其故。

translations.literal: Literal: Though Chen was annexed by Chu, its celestial assignment remained as before.
translations.idiomatic: Though Chen was annexed by Chu, its celestial assignment remained as before.

sentences.zh: 潁川、南陽，本夏禹之國。

translations.literal: Literal: Yingchuan and Nanyang had originally been the domain of Xia Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Yingchuan and Nanyang had originally been the domain of Xia Yu.

sentences.zh: 夏人上忠，其敝鄙朴。

translations.literal: Literal: The Xia people prized loyalty; in decline this became rustic simplicity.
translations.idiomatic: The Xia people prized loyalty; in decline this became rustic simplicity.

sentences.zh: 韓自武子後七世稱侯，六世稱王，五世而為秦所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: From Han Wuzi onward, seven generations later the line took the marquis title, six generations later called itself king, and five generations later was destroyed by Qin.
translations.idiomatic: From Han Wuzi onward, seven generations later the line took the marquis title, six generations later called itself king, and five generations later was destroyed by Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦既滅韓，徙天下不軌之民於南陽，故其俗夸奢，上氣力，好商賈漁獵，藏匿難制御也。

translations.literal: Literal: After Qin destroyed Han, it moved unruly people from across the empire into Nanyang. As a result, local customs became boastful and extravagant, admired brute force, favored trade and hunting, and harbored lawless groups that were hard to control.
translations.idiomatic: After Qin destroyed Han, it moved unruly people from across the empire into Nanyang. As a result, local customs became boastful and extravagant, admired brute force, favored trade and hunting, and harbored lawless groups that were hard to control.

sentences.zh: 宛，西通武關，東受江、淮，一都之會也。

translations.literal: Literal: Wan connected westward through Wuguan and eastward to the Jiang and Huai regions; it was a major metropolitan hub.
translations.idiomatic: Wan connected westward through Wuguan and eastward to the Jiang and Huai regions; it was a major metropolitan hub.

sentences.zh: 宣帝時，鄭弘、召信臣為南陽太守，治皆見紀。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Xuan’s reign, Zheng Hong and Zhao Xinchen served as administrators of Nanyang, and both left notable records of governance.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Xuan’s reign, Zheng Hong and Zhao Xinchen served as administrators of Nanyang, and both left notable records of governance.

sentences.zh: 信臣勸民農桑，去末歸本，郡以殷富。

translations.literal: Literal: Xinchen encouraged agriculture and sericulture, drew people away from secondary pursuits, and returned them to primary production, making the commandery prosperous.
translations.idiomatic: Xinchen encouraged agriculture and sericulture, drew people away from secondary pursuits, and returned them to primary production, making the commandery prosperous.

sentences.zh: 潁川，韓都。

translations.literal: Literal: Yingchuan had been the Han capital region.
translations.idiomatic: Yingchuan had been the Han capital region.

sentences.zh: 士有申子、韓非，刻害餘烈，高士宦，好文法，民以貪遴爭訟生分為失。

translations.literal: Literal: Its intellectual line included Shen Buhai and Han Fei. Their harsh legal legacy endured: officials esteemed office, valued statutes and techniques, and among the people greed, litigiousness, and factional division were considered social failings.
translations.idiomatic: Its intellectual line included Shen Buhai and Han Fei. Their harsh legal legacy endured: officials esteemed office, valued statutes and techniques, and among the people greed, litigiousness, and factional division were considered social failings.

sentences.zh: 韓延壽為太守，先之以敬讓；

translations.literal: Literal: As administrator, Han Yanshou began with respect and mutual deference;
translations.idiomatic: As administrator, Han Yanshou began with respect and mutual deference;.

sentences.zh: 黃霸繼之，教化大行，獄或八年亡重罪囚。

translations.literal: Literal: Huang Ba followed him, and moral transformation flourished so fully that for as long as eight years there were no prisoners convicted of grave crimes.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Ba followed him, and moral transformation flourished so fully that for as long as eight years there were no prisoners convicted of grave crimes.

sentences.zh: 南陽好商賈，召父富以本業；

translations.literal: Literal: Nanyang favored commerce, while Lord Zhao made it wealthy through primary occupations;
translations.idiomatic: Nanyang favored commerce, while Lord Zhao made it wealthy through primary occupations;.

sentences.zh: 潁川好爭訟分異，黃、韓化以篤厚。

translations.literal: Literal: Yingchuan was prone to litigation and faction, but Huang and Han transformed it toward honesty and solidity.
translations.idiomatic: Yingchuan was prone to litigation and faction, but Huang and Han transformed it toward honesty and solidity.

sentences.zh: 「君子之德風也，小人之德草也」，信矣。

translations.literal: Literal: "The gentleman’s virtue is like wind; the petty man’s virtue is like grass"—this is indeed true.
translations.idiomatic: "The gentleman’s virtue is like wind; the petty man’s virtue is like grass"—this is indeed true.

sentences.zh: 自東井六度至亢六度，謂之壽星之次，鄭之分野，與韓同分。

translations.literal: Literal: From the sixth degree of Eastern Well to the sixth degree of Kang is called the Longevity-Star station, the celestial division of Zheng, shared with Han.
translations.idiomatic: From the sixth degree of Eastern Well to the sixth degree of Kang is called the Longevity-Star station, the celestial division of Zheng, shared with Han.

sentences.zh: 趙地，昴、畢之分野。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zhao region corresponds to the stellar fields of Mao and Bi.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhao region corresponds to the stellar fields of Mao and Bi.

sentences.zh: 趙分晉，得趙國。

translations.literal: Literal: Zhao split off from Jin and received the Zhao state domain.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao split off from Jin and received the Zhao state domain.

sentences.zh: 北有信都、真定、常山、中山，又得涿郡之高陽、鄚、州鄉；

translations.literal: Literal: To the north it included Xindu, Zhending, Changshan, and Zhongshan, and also Gaoyang, Mo, and Zhouxiang in Zhuo Commandery;
translations.idiomatic: To the north it included Xindu, Zhending, Changshan, and Zhongshan, and also Gaoyang, Mo, and Zhouxiang in Zhuo Commandery;.

sentences.zh: 東有廣平、鉅鹿、清河、河間，又得渤海郡之東平舒、中邑、文安、束州、成平、章武，河以北也；

translations.literal: Literal: to the east it included Guangping, Julu, Qinghe, and Hejian, and also Dongpingshu, Zhongyi, Wen’an, Shuzhou, Chengping, and Zhangwu in Bohai Commandery, all north of the river;
translations.idiomatic: To the east it included Guangping, Julu, Qinghe, and Hejian, and also Dongpingshu, Zhongyi, Wen’an, Shuzhou, Chengping, and Zhangwu in Bohai Commandery, all north of the river.

sentences.zh: 南至浮水、繁陽、內黃、斥丘；

translations.literal: Literal: to the south it reached Fushui, Fanyang, Neihuang, and Chiqiu;
translations.idiomatic: To the south it reached Fushui, Fanyang, Neihuang, and Chiqiu.

sentences.zh: 西有太原、定襄、雲中、五原、上黨。

translations.literal: Literal: to the west it included Taiyuan, Dingxiang, Yunzhong, Wuyuan, and Shangdang.
translations.idiomatic: To the west it included Taiyuan, Dingxiang, Yunzhong, Wuyuan, and Shangdang.

sentences.zh: 上黨，本韓之別郡也，遠韓近趙，後卒降趙，皆趙分也。

translations.literal: Literal: Shangdang had originally been a detached Han commandery. Distant from Han and close to Zhao, it eventually surrendered to Zhao, so all of it counted as Zhao territory.
translations.idiomatic: Shangdang had originally been a detached Han commandery. Distant from Han and close to Zhao, it eventually surrendered to Zhao, so all of it counted as Zhao territory.

sentences.zh: 自趙夙後九世稱侯，四世敬侯徙都邯鄲，至曾孫武靈王稱王，五世為秦所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: Nine generations after Zhao Su, the line took the marquis title. In the fourth generation, Marquis Jing moved the capital to Handan; by his great-grandson King Wuling, they claimed kingship. Five generations later Zhao was destroyed by Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Nine generations after Zhao Su, the line took the marquis title. In the fourth generation, Marquis Jing moved the capital to Handan; by his great-grandson King Wuling, they claimed kingship. Five generations later Zhao was destroyed by Qin.

sentences.zh: 趙、中山地薄人眾，猶有沙丘紂淫亂餘民。

translations.literal: Literal: In Zhao and Zhongshan, the land was thin and population dense, and remnants of the licentious ways associated with Shaqiu and King Zhou still persisted.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhao and Zhongshan, the land was thin and population dense, and remnants of the licentious ways associated with Shaqiu and King Zhou still persisted.

sentences.zh: 丈夫相聚游戲，悲歌慷慨，起則椎剽掘冢，作姦巧，多弄物，為倡優。

translations.literal: Literal: Men gathered for games and sang tragic, impassioned songs; when stirred, they turned to raiding, grave-robbing, and crafty wrongdoing, often becoming entertainers and tricksters.
translations.idiomatic: Men gathered for games and sang tragic, impassioned songs; when stirred, they turned to raiding, grave-robbing, and crafty wrongdoing, often becoming entertainers and tricksters.

sentences.zh: 女子彈弦跕硔，游媚富貴，遍諸侯之後宮。

translations.literal: Literal: Women played strings and percussion, sought favor among the wealthy and powerful, and circulated through the rear palaces of many feudal courts.
translations.idiomatic: Women played strings and percussion, sought favor among the wealthy and powerful, and circulated through the rear palaces of many feudal courts.

sentences.zh: 邯鄲北通燕、涿，南有鄭、衛，漳、河之間一都會也。

translations.literal: Literal: Handan linked northward to Yan and Zhuo, and southward to Zheng and Wei; between the Zhang and Yellow rivers it was a great urban center.
translations.idiomatic: Handan linked northward to Yan and Zhuo, and southward to Zheng and Wei; between the Zhang and Yellow rivers it was a great urban center.

sentences.zh: 其土廣俗雜，大率精急，高氣勢，輕為姦。

translations.literal: Literal: Its territory was broad and customs mixed, but in general people were sharp, quick-tempered, status-conscious, and easily drawn to wrongdoing.
translations.idiomatic: Its territory was broad and customs mixed, but in general people were sharp, quick-tempered, status-conscious, and easily drawn to wrongdoing.

sentences.zh: 太原、上黨又多晉公族子孫，以詐力相傾，矜夸功名，報仇過直，嫁取送死奢靡。

translations.literal: Literal: Taiyuan and Shangdang also had many descendants of Jin noble houses; they undercut one another through guile and force, boasted of merit and reputation, took revenge beyond proper bounds, and spent extravagantly on marriages and funerals.
translations.idiomatic: Taiyuan and Shangdang also had many descendants of Jin noble houses; they undercut one another through guile and force, boasted of merit and reputation, took revenge beyond proper bounds, and spent extravagantly on marriages and funerals.

sentences.zh: 漢興，號為難治，常擇嚴猛之將，或任殺伐為威。

translations.literal: Literal: After Han was founded, this region was considered difficult to govern, so the court often chose severe and forceful generals, some of whom relied on executions to establish authority.
translations.idiomatic: After Han was founded, this region was considered difficult to govern, so the court often chose severe and forceful generals, some of whom relied on executions to establish authority.

sentences.zh: 父兄被誅，子弟怨憤，至告訐刺史二千石，或報殺其親屬。

translations.literal: Literal: When fathers or elder brothers were executed, younger kin often nursed resentment, sometimes denouncing inspectors and high officials, and sometimes retaliating by killing their relatives.
translations.idiomatic: When fathers or elder brothers were executed, younger kin often nursed resentment, sometimes denouncing inspectors and high officials, and sometimes retaliating by killing their relatives.

sentences.zh: 鍾、代、石、北，迫近胡寇，民俗懻忮，好氣為姦，不事農商，自全晉時，已患其剽悍，而武靈王又益厲之。

translations.literal: Literal: In Zhongshan, Dai, Shi, and Bei, near frequent Hu raids, local customs were contentious and aggressive. People prized bravado and wrongdoing, neglected farming and trade, and had long suffered from a hard, predatory temperament even in old Jin times; King Wuling only intensified it.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongshan, Dai, Shi, and Bei, near frequent Hu raids, local customs were contentious and aggressive. People prized bravado and wrongdoing, neglected farming and trade, and had long suffered from a hard, predatory temperament even in old Jin times; King Wuling only intensified it.

sentences.zh: 故冀州之部，盜賊常為它州劇。

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore, within Jizhou, this region was often the most troubled by banditry.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore, within Jizhou, this region was often the most troubled by banditry.

sentences.zh: 定襄、雲中、五原，本戎狄地，頗有趙、齊、衛、楚之徙。

translations.literal: Literal: Dingxiang, Yunzhong, and Wuyuan had originally been Rong and Di lands, though they also contained migrants from Zhao, Qi, Wei, and Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Dingxiang, Yunzhong, and Wuyuan had originally been Rong and Di lands, though they also contained migrants from Zhao, Qi, Wei, and Chu.

sentences.zh: 其民鄙朴，少禮文，好射獵。

translations.literal: Literal: Their people were rustic and plain, with little concern for ritual learning, and they favored archery and hunting.
translations.idiomatic: Their people were rustic and plain, with little concern for ritual learning, and they favored archery and hunting.

sentences.zh: 雁門亦同俗，於天文別屬燕。

translations.literal: Literal: Yanmen shared these same customs, though in celestial terms it was assigned separately to Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Yanmen shared these same customs, though in celestial terms it was assigned separately to Yan.

sentences.zh: 燕地，尾、箕分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Yan region corresponds to the stellar fields of Tail and Basket.
translations.idiomatic: The Yan region corresponds to the stellar fields of Tail and Basket.

sentences.zh: 武王定殷，封召公於燕，其後三十六世與六國俱稱王。

translations.literal: Literal: After King Wu settled Shang, he enfeoffed Duke Shao in Yan; thirty-six generations later, Yan too took the title of king along with the other six states.
translations.idiomatic: After King Wu settled Shang, he enfeoffed Duke Shao in Yan; thirty-six generations later, Yan too took the title of king along with the other six states.

sentences.zh: 東有漁陽、右北平、遼西，遼東，西有上谷、代郡、雁門，南得涿郡之易、容城、范陽、北新城、故安、涿縣、良鄉、新昌，及勃海之安次，皆燕分也。

translations.literal: Literal: To the east it held Yuyang, Youbeiping, Liaoxi, and Liaodong; to the west Shanggu, Dai, and Yanmen; to the south Yi, Rongcheng, Fanyang, Beixincheng, Gu’an, Zhuoxian, Liangxiang, and Xinchang in Zhuo Commandery, plus Anci in Bohai. All these belonged to Yan’s division.
translations.idiomatic: To the east it held Yuyang, Youbeiping, Liaoxi, and Liaodong; to the west Shanggu, Dai, and Yanmen; to the south Yi, Rongcheng, Fanyang, Beixincheng, Gu’an, Zhuoxian, Liangxiang, and Xinchang in Zhuo Commandery, plus Anci in Bohai. All these belonged to Yan’s division.

sentences.zh: 樂浪、玄菟，亦宜屬焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Lelang and Xuantu should likewise be included under it.
translations.idiomatic: Lelang and Xuantu should likewise be included under it.

sentences.zh: 燕稱王十世，秦欲滅六國，燕王太子丹遣勇士荊軻西刺秦王，不成而誅，秦遂舉兵滅燕。

translations.literal: Literal: Yan ruled as a kingdom for ten generations. When Qin sought to destroy the six states, Crown Prince Dan of Yan sent the warrior Jing Ke west to assassinate the Qin king. He failed and was executed; Qin then sent troops and destroyed Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Yan ruled as a kingdom for ten generations. When Qin sought to destroy the six states, Crown Prince Dan of Yan sent the warrior Jing Ke west to assassinate the Qin king. He failed and was executed; Qin then sent troops and destroyed Yan.

sentences.zh: 薊，南通齊、趙，勃、碣之間一都會也。

translations.literal: Literal: Ji connected southward to Qi and Zhao and was a major metropolitan center between Bohai and Jieshi.
translations.idiomatic: Ji connected southward to Qi and Zhao and was a major metropolitan center between Bohai and Jieshi.

sentences.zh: 初太子丹賓養勇士，不愛後宮美女，民化以為俗，至今猶然。

translations.literal: Literal: At first, Crown Prince Dan hosted and supported men of daring and did not begrudge palace women; people took this as a model of custom, and traces of it remained down to recent times.
translations.idiomatic: At first, Crown Prince Dan hosted and supported men of daring and did not begrudge palace women; people took this as a model of custom, and traces of it remained down to recent times.

sentences.zh: 賓客相過，以婦侍宿，嫁取之夕，男女無別，反以為榮。

translations.literal: Literal: Guests visiting one another were attended at night by women; at weddings men and women were scarcely distinguished, and this was treated as honorable.
translations.idiomatic: Guests visiting one another were attended at night by women; at weddings men and women were scarcely distinguished, and this was treated as honorable.

sentences.zh: 後稍頗止，然終未改。

translations.literal: Literal: Later this was somewhat restrained, but it was never fully changed.
translations.idiomatic: Later this was somewhat restrained, but it was never fully changed.

sentences.zh: 其俗愚悍少慮，輕薄無威，亦有所長，敢於急人，燕丹遺風也。

translations.literal: Literal: Their customs were often blunt and fierce, with little foresight and light regard for authority; yet they also had strengths, especially a readiness to aid others in emergencies, a legacy of Prince Dan of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Their customs were often blunt and fierce, with little foresight and light regard for authority; yet they also had strengths, especially a readiness to aid others in emergencies, a legacy of Prince Dan of Yan.

sentences.zh: 上谷至遼東，地廣民希，數被胡寇，俗與趙、代相類，有魚鹽棗栗之饒。

translations.literal: Literal: From Shanggu to Liaodong, the land was broad and population sparse, often raided by Hu tribes. Customs resembled those of Zhao and Dai, and the region was rich in fish, salt, dates, and chestnuts.
translations.idiomatic: From Shanggu to Liaodong, the land was broad and population sparse, often raided by Hu tribes. Customs resembled those of Zhao and Dai, and the region was rich in fish, salt, dates, and chestnuts.

sentences.zh: 北隙烏丸、夫餘，東賈真番之利。

translations.literal: Literal: Through northern routes it traded with Wuhuan and Fuyu, and eastward it profited from commerce with Zhenfan.
translations.idiomatic: Through northern routes it traded with Wuhuan and Fuyu, and eastward it profited from commerce with Zhenfan.

sentences.zh: 玄菟、樂浪，武帝時置，皆朝鮮、濊貉、句驪蠻夷。

translations.literal: Literal: Xuantu and Lelang were established in Emperor Wu’s reign; their populations were largely Korean, Yemaek, Goguryeo, and other frontier peoples.
translations.idiomatic: Xuantu and Lelang were established in Emperor Wu’s reign; their populations were largely Korean, Yemaek, Goguryeo, and other frontier peoples.

sentences.zh: 殷道衰，箕子去之朝鮮，教其民以禮義，田蠶織作。

translations.literal: Literal: When Shang’s Way declined, Jizi departed to Joseon and taught the people rites, righteousness, agriculture, sericulture, and weaving.
translations.idiomatic: When Shang’s Way declined, Jizi departed to Joseon and taught the people rites, righteousness, agriculture, sericulture, and weaving.

sentences.zh: 樂浪朝鮮民犯禁八條：相殺以當時償殺；

translations.literal: Literal: Among the people of Joseon in Lelang there were eight prohibitions: those who killed had to pay with their own lives immediately;
translations.idiomatic: Among the people of Joseon in Lelang there were eight prohibitions: those who killed had to pay with their own lives immediately;.

sentences.zh: 相傷以穀償；

translations.literal: Literal: those who injured others compensated with grain;
translations.idiomatic: Those who injured others compensated with grain.

sentences.zh: 相盜者男沒入為其家奴，女子為婢，欲自贖者，人五十萬。

translations.literal: Literal: thieves were made household slaves if male and maidservants if female; those who wished to redeem themselves paid 500,000.
translations.idiomatic: Thieves were made household slaves if male and maidservants if female; those who wished to redeem themselves paid 500,000.

sentences.zh: 雖免為民，俗猶羞之，嫁取無所讎，是以其民終不相盜，無門戶之閉，婦人貞信不淫辟。

translations.literal: Literal: Even after regaining commoner status, people remained ashamed of such offenses. Marriage partners were hard to find for them. Thus theft was rare, households needed no barred gates, and women were chaste and trustworthy, not licentious.
translations.idiomatic: Even after regaining commoner status, people remained ashamed of such offenses. Marriage partners were hard to find for them. Thus theft was rare, households needed no barred gates, and women were chaste and trustworthy, not licentious.

sentences.zh: 其田民飲食以籩豆，都邑頗放效吏及內郡賈人，往往以杯器食。

translations.literal: Literal: In fields and daily meals the people used ritual vessels like bian and dou, while in cities many imitated officials and inner-commandery merchants and often ate from cup-like utensils.
translations.idiomatic: In fields and daily meals the people used ritual vessels like bian and dou, while in cities many imitated officials and inner-commandery merchants and often ate from cup-like utensils.

sentences.zh: 郡初取吏於遼東，吏見民無閉臧，及賈人往者，夜則為盜，俗稍益薄。

translations.literal: Literal: At first the commanderies recruited officials from Liaodong. Seeing that locals left homes unsecured, some officials and visiting merchants stole at night, and customs gradually deteriorated.
translations.idiomatic: At first the commanderies recruited officials from Liaodong. Seeing that locals left homes unsecured, some officials and visiting merchants stole at night, and customs gradually deteriorated.

sentences.zh: 今於犯禁浸多，至六十餘條。

translations.literal: Literal: Now the number of prohibited offenses has increased greatly, to more than sixty.
translations.idiomatic: Now the number of prohibited offenses has increased greatly, to more than sixty.

sentences.zh: 可貴哉，仁賢之化也！

translations.literal: Literal: How precious the transforming power of benevolent and worthy governance is!
translations.idiomatic: How precious the transforming power of benevolent and worthy governance is!

sentences.zh: 然東夷天性柔順，異於三方之外，故孔子悼道不行，設浮於海，欲居九夷，有以也夫！

translations.literal: Literal: Yet the Eastern Yi are by nature gentle and compliant, unlike the peoples beyond the other three directions. So when Confucius lamented that the Way was not practiced and said he wished to voyage by raft and dwell among the Nine Yi, he had his reasons.
translations.idiomatic: Yet the Eastern Yi are by nature gentle and compliant, unlike the peoples beyond the other three directions. So when Confucius lamented that the Way was not practiced and said he wished to voyage by raft and dwell among the Nine Yi, he had his reasons.

sentences.zh: 樂浪海中有倭人，分為百餘國，以歲時來獻見云。

translations.literal: Literal: In the sea off Lelang there are Wa people, divided into more than a hundred states, who are said to come at fixed seasons to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the sea off Lelang there are Wa people, divided into more than a hundred states, who are said to come at fixed seasons to present tribute.

sentences.zh: 自危四度至斗六度，謂之析木之次，燕之分也。

translations.literal: Literal: From the fourth degree of Wei to the sixth degree of Dou is called the Split-Wood station; this is Yan’s division.
translations.idiomatic: From the fourth degree of Wei to the sixth degree of Dou is called the Split-Wood station; this is Yan’s division.

sentences.zh: 齊地，虛、危之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Qi region corresponds to the stellar fields of Xu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: The Qi region corresponds to the stellar fields of Xu and Wei.

sentences.zh: 東有甾川、東萊、琅邪、高密、膠東，南有泰山、城陽，北有千乘，清河以南，勃海之高樂、高城、重合、陽信，西有濟南、平原，皆齊分也。

translations.literal: Literal: To the east it included Zichuan, Donglai, Langya, Gaomi, and Jiaodong; to the south Taishan and Chengyang; to the north Qiancheng; and south of Qinghe, Gaole, Gaocheng, Chonghe, and Yangxin in Bohai; to the west Jinan and Pingyuan. All these belonged to Qi’s division.
translations.idiomatic: To the east it included Zichuan, Donglai, Langya, Gaomi, and Jiaodong; to the south Taishan and Chengyang; to the north Qiancheng; and south of Qinghe, Gaole, Gaocheng, Chonghe, and Yangxin in Bohai; to the west Jinan and Pingyuan. All these belonged to Qi’s division.

sentences.zh: 少昊之世有爽鳩氏，虞、夏時有季崱，湯時有逢公柏陵，殷末有薄姑氏，皆為諸侯，國此地。

translations.literal: Literal: In Shaohao’s time there was the Shuangjiu clan; in Yu and Xia times, Jize; in Tang’s time, Fenggong Bailing; and at the end of Shang, the Bogu clan. All were feudal lords who held states in this region.
translations.idiomatic: In Shaohao’s time there was the Shuangjiu clan; in Yu and Xia times, Jize; in Tang’s time, Fenggong Bailing; and at the end of Shang, the Bogu clan. All were feudal lords who held states in this region.

sentences.zh: 至周成王時，薄姑氏與四國共作亂，成王滅之，以封師尚父，是為太公。

translations.literal: Literal: By King Cheng of Zhou’s reign, the Bogu clan joined with four states in rebellion. King Cheng destroyed them and enfeoffed the Grand Duke, Master Shangfu, there.
translations.idiomatic: By King Cheng of Zhou’s reign, the Bogu clan joined with four states in rebellion. King Cheng destroyed them and enfeoffed the Grand Duke, Master Shangfu, there.

sentences.zh: 詩風齊國是也。

translations.literal: Literal: These are the Odes airs of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: These are the Odes airs of Qi.

sentences.zh: 臨甾名營丘，故《齊詩》曰：「子之營兮，遭我虖嶩之間兮。」

translations.literal: Literal: Linzi was also called Yingqiu; thus the Qi Odes says, "As you camped, we met between the furrows."
translations.idiomatic: Linzi was also called Yingqiu; thus the Qi Odes says, "As you camped, we met between the furrows."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「俟我於著乎而。」

translations.literal: Literal: It also says, "Wait for me at the gate-platform."
translations.idiomatic: It also says, "Wait for me at the gate-platform."

sentences.zh: 此亦其舒緩之體也。

translations.literal: Literal: This too reflects Qi’s relaxed and expansive style.
translations.idiomatic: This too reflects Qi’s relaxed and expansive style.

sentences.zh: 吳札聞齊之歌，曰：「泱泱乎，大風也哉！

translations.literal: Literal: When Wu Zha heard the songs of Qi, he said, "Vast indeed, what a great wind!
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Zha heard the songs of Qi, he said, "Vast indeed, what a great wind!

sentences.zh: 其太公乎？

translations.literal: Literal: Could this be the legacy of the Grand Duke?
translations.idiomatic: Could this be the legacy of the Grand Duke?

sentences.zh: 國未可量也。」

translations.literal: Literal: This state’s potential cannot be measured."
translations.idiomatic: This state’s potential cannot be measured."

sentences.zh: 古有分土，亡分民。

translations.literal: Literal: In antiquity, land was divided; people were not.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity, land was divided; people were not.

sentences.zh: 太公以齊地負海舄鹵，少五穀而人民寡，乃勸以女工之業，通魚鹽之利，而人物輻湊。

translations.literal: Literal: Because Qi faced the sea and had saline flats, with fewer grain resources and sparse population, the Grand Duke encouraged women’s textile work and opened profits from fish and salt; people and goods then converged there.
translations.idiomatic: Because Qi faced the sea and had saline flats, with fewer grain resources and sparse population, the Grand Duke encouraged women’s textile work and opened profits from fish and salt; people and goods then converged there.

sentences.zh: 後十四世，桓公用管仲，設輕重以富國，合諸侯成伯功，身在陪臣而取三歸。

translations.literal: Literal: Fourteen generations later, Duke Huan employed Guan Zhong, used price-and-weight policies to enrich the state, united the feudal lords and achieved hegemony, and while still formally a subject minister took the privileges of the "three returns."
translations.idiomatic: Fourteen generations later, Duke Huan employed Guan Zhong, used price-and-weight policies to enrich the state, united the feudal lords and achieved hegemony, and while still formally a subject minister took the privileges of the "three returns."

sentences.zh: 故其俗彌侈，織作冰紈綺繡純麗之物，號為冠帶衣履天下。

translations.literal: Literal: Hence local customs grew ever more luxurious, producing fine gauzes, patterned silks, and ornate embroidery; Qi became renowned across the empire for elite dress and attire.
translations.idiomatic: Hence local customs grew ever more luxurious, producing fine gauzes, patterned silks, and ornate embroidery; Qi became renowned across the empire for elite dress and attire.

sentences.zh: 初太公治齊，修道術，尊賢智，賞有功，故至今其土多好經術，矜功名，舒緩闊達而足智。

translations.literal: Literal: When the Grand Duke first governed Qi, he cultivated statecraft, honored worthy and wise men, and rewarded merit. To this day, people there tend to value classical learning, prize achievement and reputation, and are broad, unhurried, and resourceful.
translations.idiomatic: When the Grand Duke first governed Qi, he cultivated statecraft, honored worthy and wise men, and rewarded merit. To this day, people there tend to value classical learning, prize achievement and reputation, and are broad, unhurried, and resourceful.

sentences.zh: 其失夸奢朋黨，言與行繆，虛詐不情，急之則離散，緩之則放縱。

translations.literal: Literal: Its faults were boastful extravagance and factional cliques, with speech and action at odds, insincerity and deceit; under pressure people scattered, and with leniency they became unruly.
translations.idiomatic: Its faults were boastful extravagance and factional cliques, with speech and action at odds, insincerity and deceit; under pressure people scattered, and with leniency they became unruly.

sentences.zh: 始桓公兄襄公淫亂，姑姊妹不嫁，於是令國中民家長女不得嫁，名曰「巫兒」，為家主祠，嫁者不利其家，民至今以為俗。

translations.literal: Literal: At first, Duke Xiang, elder brother of Duke Huan, was licentious; aunts, sisters, and other women were not married out. He then ordered that eldest daughters in common households were not to marry and called them "wu-children," making them household ritual attendants. Marriage was thought inauspicious to the family, and this custom long persisted among the people.
translations.idiomatic: At first, Duke Xiang, elder brother of Duke Huan, was licentious; aunts, sisters, and other women were not married out. He then ordered that eldest daughters in common households were not to marry and called them "wu-children," making them household ritual attendants. Marriage was thought inauspicious to the family, and this custom long persisted among the people.

sentences.zh: 痛乎，道民之道，可不慎哉！

translations.literal: Literal: How painful it is: when guiding the people, how could one fail to be cautious?
translations.idiomatic: How painful it is: when guiding the people, how could one fail to be cautious?

sentences.zh: 昔太公始封，周公問「何以治齊？」

translations.literal: Literal: When the Grand Duke was first enfeoffed, the Duke of Zhou asked, "How will you govern Qi?"
translations.idiomatic: When the Grand Duke was first enfeoffed, the Duke of Zhou asked, "How will you govern Qi?"

sentences.zh: 太公曰：「舉賢而上功。」

translations.literal: Literal: The Grand Duke replied, "Promote the worthy and honor achievement."
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Duke replied, "Promote the worthy and honor achievement."

sentences.zh: 周公曰：「後世必有篡殺之臣。」

translations.literal: Literal: The Duke of Zhou said, "Then later ages will surely produce ministers who usurp and kill."
translations.idiomatic: The Duke of Zhou said, "Then later ages will surely produce ministers who usurp and kill."

sentences.zh: 其後二十九世為彊臣田和所滅，而和自立為齊侯。

translations.literal: Literal: Indeed, twenty-nine generations later, strong minister Tian He overthrew the ruling house and established himself as Marquis of Qi.
