translations.idiomatic: Indeed, twenty-nine generations later, strong minister Tian He overthrew the ruling house and established himself as Marquis of Qi.

sentences.zh: 初，和之先陳公子完有罪來奔齊，齊桓公以為大夫，更稱田氏。

translations.literal: Literal: Earlier, Tian He’s ancestor, Prince Wan of Chen, had fled to Qi after committing an offense. Duke Huan made him a grand officer, and his line adopted the surname Tian.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, Tian He’s ancestor, Prince Wan of Chen, had fled to Qi after committing an offense. Duke Huan made him a grand officer, and his line adopted the surname Tian.

sentences.zh: 九世至和而篡齊，至孫威王稱王，五世為秦所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: Nine generations later Tian He usurped Qi; by his grandson King Wei they claimed kingship, and five generations later they were destroyed by Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Nine generations later Tian He usurped Qi; by his grandson King Wei they claimed kingship, and five generations later they were destroyed by Qin.

sentences.zh: 臨甾，海、岱之間一都會也，其中具五民云。

translations.literal: Literal: Linzi was a great metropolis between sea and Mount Tai, gathering all five kinds of people.
translations.idiomatic: Linzi was a great metropolis between sea and Mount Tai, gathering all five kinds of people.

sentences.zh: 魯地，奎、婁之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Lu region corresponds to the stellar fields of Kui and Lou.
translations.idiomatic: The Lu region corresponds to the stellar fields of Kui and Lou.

sentences.zh: 東至東海，南有泗水，至淮，得臨淮之下相、睢陵、僮、取慮，皆魯分也。

translations.literal: Literal: To the east it reached the Eastern Sea; to the south it included the Si River down to the Huai, and took Xiaxiang, Suiling, Tong, and Qulu in Linhuai. All these belonged to Lu’s division.
translations.idiomatic: To the east it reached the Eastern Sea; to the south it included the Si River down to the Huai, and took Xiaxiang, Suiling, Tong, and Qulu in Linhuai. All these belonged to Lu’s division.

sentences.zh: 周興，以少昊之虛曲阜封周公子伯禽為魯侯，以為周公主。

translations.literal: Literal: When Zhou arose, it enfeoffed Bo Qin, son of the Duke of Zhou, at Qufu, the old seat of Shaohao, making him Marquis of Lu as the Duke of Zhou’s representative.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhou arose, it enfeoffed Bo Qin, son of the Duke of Zhou, at Qufu, the old seat of Shaohao, making him Marquis of Lu as the Duke of Zhou’s representative.

sentences.zh: 其民有聖人之教化，故孔子曰「齊一變至於魯，魯一變至於道」，言近正也。

translations.literal: Literal: Its people had the transforming influence of sages. Thus Confucius said, "If Qi changed once, it would reach Lu; if Lu changed once, it would reach the Way," meaning Lu was already close to correctness.
translations.idiomatic: Its people had the transforming influence of sages. Thus Confucius said, "If Qi changed once, it would reach Lu; if Lu changed once, it would reach the Way," meaning Lu was already close to correctness.

sentences.zh: 瀕洙泗之水，其民涉度，幼者扶老而代其任。

translations.literal: Literal: Along the Zhu and Si rivers, people crossing the water had the young support the elderly and take over their burdens.
translations.idiomatic: Along the Zhu and Si rivers, people crossing the water had the young support the elderly and take over their burdens.

sentences.zh: 俗既益薄，長老不自安，與幼少相讓，故曰：「魯道衰，洙泗之間齗齗如也。」

translations.literal: Literal: As customs gradually thinned, elders no longer felt secure, and old and young deferred to one another only superficially; hence the saying, "When the Way of Lu declined, people between the Zhu and Si bickered face to face."
translations.idiomatic: As customs gradually thinned, elders no longer felt secure, and old and young deferred to one another only superficially; hence the saying, "When the Way of Lu declined, people between the Zhu and Si bickered face to face."

sentences.zh: 孔子閔王道將廢，乃修六經，以述唐虞三代之道，弟子受業而通者七十有七人。

translations.literal: Literal: When Confucius saw kingly governance about to disappear, he edited the Six Classics to transmit the Way of Tang, Yu, and the Three Dynasties; among his disciples, seventy-seven mastered the teachings.
translations.idiomatic: When Confucius saw kingly governance about to disappear, he edited the Six Classics to transmit the Way of Tang, Yu, and the Three Dynasties; among his disciples, seventy-seven mastered the teachings.

sentences.zh: 是以其民好學，上禮義，重廉恥。

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore its people valued learning, honored rites and righteousness, and esteemed integrity and shame.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore its people valued learning, honored rites and righteousness, and esteemed integrity and shame.

sentences.zh: 周公始封，太公問「何以治魯？」

translations.literal: Literal: When the Duke of Zhou was first enfeoffed, the Grand Duke asked, "How will you govern Lu?"
translations.idiomatic: When the Duke of Zhou was first enfeoffed, the Grand Duke asked, "How will you govern Lu?"

sentences.zh: 周公曰：「尊尊而親親。」

translations.literal: Literal: The Duke of Zhou replied, "Honor the honored, and be close to kin."
translations.idiomatic: The Duke of Zhou replied, "Honor the honored, and be close to kin."

sentences.zh: 太公曰：「後世浸弱矣。」

translations.literal: Literal: The Grand Duke said, "Then in later ages it will gradually weaken."
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Duke said, "Then in later ages it will gradually weaken."

sentences.zh: 故魯自文公以後，祿去公室，政在大夫，季氏逐昭公，陵夷微弱，三十四世而為楚所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: So after Duke Wen of Lu, emoluments left the ducal house and power fell to great officers. The Ji clan expelled Duke Zhao, and Lu declined into weakness; after thirty-four generations it was destroyed by Chu.
translations.idiomatic: So after Duke Wen of Lu, emoluments left the ducal house and power fell to great officers. The Ji clan expelled Duke Zhao, and Lu declined into weakness; after thirty-four generations it was destroyed by Chu.

sentences.zh: 然本大國，故自為分野。

translations.literal: Literal: Yet because it had once been a great state, it retained its own regional division.
translations.idiomatic: Yet because it had once been a great state, it retained its own regional division.

sentences.zh: 今去聖久遠，周公遺化銷微，孔氏庠序衰壞。

translations.literal: Literal: Now the sages are far removed, the Duke of Zhou’s legacy has grown faint, and the Kong clan’s schools have decayed.
translations.idiomatic: Now the sages are far removed, the Duke of Zhou’s legacy has grown faint, and the Kong clan’s schools have decayed.

sentences.zh: 地骥民眾，頗有桑麻之業，亡林澤之饒。

translations.literal: Literal: The land is narrow and the people numerous, with some mulberry and hemp production but little wealth from forests and marshes.
translations.idiomatic: The land is narrow and the people numerous, with some mulberry and hemp production but little wealth from forests and marshes.

sentences.zh: 俗儉嗇愛財，趨商賈，好訾毀，多巧偽，喪祭之禮文備實寡，然其好學猶愈於它俗。

translations.literal: Literal: Customs are frugal and tightfisted, people love wealth and turn to commerce, relish slander, and rely on clever deceit. Funeral and sacrificial forms are elaborate while substance is thin; still, their devotion to study remains better than in many other regions.
translations.idiomatic: Customs are frugal and tightfisted, people love wealth and turn to commerce, relish slander, and rely on clever deceit. Funeral and sacrificial forms are elaborate while substance is thin; still, their devotion to study remains better than in many other regions.

sentences.zh: 漢興以來，魯東海多至卿相。

translations.literal: Literal: Since the Han founding, Lu and Donghai have produced many high ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Since the Han founding, Lu and Donghai have produced many high ministers.

sentences.zh: 東平、須昌、壽良，皆在濟東，屬魯，非宋地也，當考。

translations.literal: Literal: Dongping, Xuchang, and Shouliang all lie east of the Ji and belonged to Lu, not Song; this should be carefully examined.
translations.idiomatic: Dongping, Xuchang, and Shouliang all lie east of the Ji and belonged to Lu, not Song; this should be carefully examined.

sentences.zh: 宋地，房、心之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Song region corresponds to the stellar fields of Fang and Xin.
translations.idiomatic: The Song region corresponds to the stellar fields of Fang and Xin.

sentences.zh: 今之沛、梁、楚、山陽、濟陰、東平及東郡之須昌、壽張，皆宋分也。

translations.literal: Literal: Present-day Pei, Liang, Chu, Shanyang, Jiyin, Dongping, and Xuchang and Shouzhang in Dong Commandery all belong to Song’s division.
translations.idiomatic: Present-day Pei, Liang, Chu, Shanyang, Jiyin, Dongping, and Xuchang and Shouzhang in Dong Commandery all belong to Song’s division.

sentences.zh: 周封微子於宋，今之睢陽是也，本陶唐氏火正閼伯之虛也。

translations.literal: Literal: Zhou enfeoffed Weizi at Song, at what is now Suiyang; this had originally been the old seat of Ebo, Fire Official of the Tao Tang line.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou enfeoffed Weizi at Song, at what is now Suiyang; this had originally been the old seat of Ebo, Fire Official of the Tao Tang line.

sentences.zh: 濟陰定陶，詩風曹國也。

translations.literal: Literal: Dingtao in Jiyin belonged to the Odes air of Cao.
translations.idiomatic: Dingtao in Jiyin belonged to the Odes air of Cao.

sentences.zh: 武王封弟叔振鐸於曹，其後稍大，得山陽、陳留，二十餘世為宋所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: King Wu enfeoffed his younger brother Shu Zhenduo at Cao. The state later expanded, taking Shanyang and Chenliu, and after more than twenty generations it was destroyed by Song.
translations.idiomatic: King Wu enfeoffed his younger brother Shu Zhenduo at Cao. The state later expanded, taking Shanyang and Chenliu, and after more than twenty generations it was destroyed by Song.

sentences.zh: 昔堯作游成陽，舜漁剨澤，湯止于亳，故其民猶有先王遺風，重厚多君子，好稼穡，惡衣食，以致畜藏。

translations.literal: Literal: In antiquity Yao traveled through Chengyang, Shun fished at Huo Marsh, and Tang halted at Bo. Thus the people retained vestiges of former-kings’ ways: they were steady and substantial, produced many gentlemen, valued farming, cared little for luxury in food and clothing, and accumulated stores.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity Yao traveled through Chengyang, Shun fished at Huo Marsh, and Tang halted at Bo. Thus the people retained vestiges of former-kings’ ways: they were steady and substantial, produced many gentlemen, valued farming, cared little for luxury in food and clothing, and accumulated stores.

sentences.zh: 宋自微子二十餘世，至景公滅曹，滅曹後五世亦為齊、楚、魏所滅，參分其地。

translations.literal: Literal: More than twenty generations after Weizi, Song under Duke Jing destroyed Cao; five generations later Song itself was destroyed by Qi, Chu, and Wei, which split its lands.
translations.idiomatic: More than twenty generations after Weizi, Song under Duke Jing destroyed Cao; five generations later Song itself was destroyed by Qi, Chu, and Wei, which split its lands.

sentences.zh: 魏得其梁、陳留，齊得其濟陰、東平，楚得其沛。

translations.literal: Literal: Wei took Liang and Chenliu; Qi took Jiyin and Dongping; Chu took Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Wei took Liang and Chenliu; Qi took Jiyin and Dongping; Chu took Pei.

sentences.zh: 故今之楚彭城，本宋也，春秋經曰「圍宋彭城」。

translations.literal: Literal: Thus present-day Pengcheng in Chu territory had originally been Song land, as the Spring and Autumn states: "They besieged Song at Pengcheng."
translations.idiomatic: Thus present-day Pengcheng in Chu territory had originally been Song land, as the Spring and Autumn states: "They besieged Song at Pengcheng."

sentences.zh: 宋雖滅，本大國，故自為分野。

translations.literal: Literal: Though Song was destroyed, it had been a great state, so it retains its own regional division.
translations.idiomatic: Though Song was destroyed, it had been a great state, so it retains its own regional division.

sentences.zh: 沛楚之失，急疾顓己，地薄民貧，而山陽好為姦盜。

translations.literal: Literal: The faults of Pei and Chu were impulsive self-assertion; the land was poor and people impoverished, while Shanyang was notorious for banditry.
translations.idiomatic: The faults of Pei and Chu were impulsive self-assertion; the land was poor and people impoverished, while Shanyang was notorious for banditry.

sentences.zh: 衛地，營室、東壁之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Wei region corresponds to the stellar fields of Encampment and Eastern Wall.
translations.idiomatic: The Wei region corresponds to the stellar fields of Encampment and Eastern Wall.

sentences.zh: 今之東郡及魏郡黎陽，河內之野王、朝歌，皆衛分也。

translations.literal: Literal: Present-day Dong Commandery, Liyang in Wei Commandery, and Yewang and Chaoge in Henei all belong to Wei’s division.
translations.idiomatic: Present-day Dong Commandery, Liyang in Wei Commandery, and Yewang and Chaoge in Henei all belong to Wei’s division.

sentences.zh: 衛本國既為狄所滅，文公徙封楚丘，三十餘年，子成公徙於帝丘。

translations.literal: Literal: After the original state of Wei was destroyed by the Di, Duke Wen was re-enfeoffed at Chuqiu; after more than thirty years, his son Duke Cheng moved to Diqiu.
translations.idiomatic: After the original state of Wei was destroyed by the Di, Duke Wen was re-enfeoffed at Chuqiu; after more than thirty years, his son Duke Cheng moved to Diqiu.

sentences.zh: 故春秋經曰「衛俣于帝丘」，今之濮陽是也。

translations.literal: Literal: Hence the Spring and Autumn says, "Wei moved to Diqiu"; this is present-day Puyang.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the Spring and Autumn says, "Wei moved to Diqiu"; this is present-day Puyang.

sentences.zh: 本顓頊之虛，故謂之帝丘。

translations.literal: Literal: It was originally the old seat of Zhuanxu, and thus was called Diqiu.
translations.idiomatic: It was originally the old seat of Zhuanxu, and thus was called Diqiu.

sentences.zh: 夏后之世，昆吾氏居之。

translations.literal: Literal: In Xia times, the Kunwu clan lived there.
translations.idiomatic: In Xia times, the Kunwu clan lived there.

sentences.zh: 成公後十餘世，為韓、魏所侵，盡亡其旁邑，獨有濮陽。

translations.literal: Literal: More than ten generations after Duke Cheng, Wei was encroached on by Han and Wei until all outlying towns were lost, leaving only Puyang.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten generations after Duke Cheng, Wei was encroached on by Han and Wei until all outlying towns were lost, leaving only Puyang.

sentences.zh: 後秦滅濮陽，置東郡，徙之於野王。

translations.literal: Literal: Later Qin destroyed Puyang, established Dong Commandery, and relocated the Wei ruler to Yewang.
translations.idiomatic: Later Qin destroyed Puyang, established Dong Commandery, and relocated the Wei ruler to Yewang.

sentences.zh: 始皇既并天下，猶獨置衛君，二世時乃廢為庶人。

translations.literal: Literal: After the First Emperor unified the realm, he still left a Wei ruler in place; under the Second Emperor he was finally demoted to commoner status.
translations.idiomatic: After the First Emperor unified the realm, he still left a Wei ruler in place; under the Second Emperor he was finally demoted to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 凡四十世，九百年，最後絕，故獨為分野。

translations.literal: Literal: In total the line lasted forty generations and nine hundred years before final extinction; therefore it alone remained a separate regional division.
translations.idiomatic: In total the line lasted forty generations and nine hundred years before final extinction; therefore it alone remained a separate regional division.

sentences.zh: 衛地有桑間濮上之阻，男女亦亟聚會，聲色生焉，故俗稱鄭衛之音。

translations.literal: Literal: In Wei lands, the barriers of Sangjian and Pushang fostered frequent gatherings of men and women; sensual songs emerged there, so this style came to be called the "music of Zheng and Wei."
translations.idiomatic: In Wei lands, the barriers of Sangjian and Pushang fostered frequent gatherings of men and women; sensual songs emerged there, so this style came to be called the "music of Zheng and Wei."

sentences.zh: 周末有子路、夏育，民人慕之，故其俗剛武，上氣力。

translations.literal: Literal: At the end of Zhou there were men like Zilu and Xiayu; people admired them, so local customs became tough and martial, honoring physical force.
translations.idiomatic: At the end of Zhou there were men like Zilu and Xiayu; people admired them, so local customs became tough and martial, honoring physical force.

sentences.zh: 漢興，二千石治者亦以殺戮為威。

translations.literal: Literal: After Han was founded, even two-thousand-shi administrators there often ruled through the threat of execution.
translations.idiomatic: After Han was founded, even two-thousand-shi administrators there often ruled through the threat of execution.

sentences.zh: 宣帝時韓延壽為東郡太守，承聖恩，崇禮義，尊諫爭，至今東郡號善為吏，延壽之化也。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Xuan’s reign, Han Yanshou served as administrator of Dong Commandery. Upholding imperial grace, he promoted rites and righteousness and honored remonstrance; to this day Dong Commandery is known for capable officials, a legacy of Han Yanshou’s transformation.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Xuan’s reign, Han Yanshou served as administrator of Dong Commandery. Upholding imperial grace, he promoted rites and righteousness and honored remonstrance; to this day Dong Commandery is known for capable officials, a legacy of Han Yanshou’s transformation.

sentences.zh: 其失頗奢靡，嫁取送死過度，而野王好氣任俠，有濮上風。

translations.literal: Literal: Its faults tended toward extravagance: weddings and funerals were excessive, and in Yewang people prized bravado and knight-errant conduct, retaining the old Pushang style.
translations.idiomatic: Its faults tended toward extravagance: weddings and funerals were excessive, and in Yewang people prized bravado and knight-errant conduct, retaining the old Pushang style.

sentences.zh: 楚地，翼、軫之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Chu region corresponds to the stellar fields of Yi and Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: The Chu region corresponds to the stellar fields of Yi and Zhen.

sentences.zh: 今之南郡、江夏、零陵、桂陽、武陵、長沙及漢中、汝南郡，盡楚分也。

translations.literal: Literal: Present-day Nanjun, Jiangxia, Lingling, Guiyang, Wuling, and Changsha, together with Hanzhong and Runan, all belonged to Chu’s division.
translations.idiomatic: Present-day Nanjun, Jiangxia, Lingling, Guiyang, Wuling, and Changsha, together with Hanzhong and Runan, all belonged to Chu’s division.

sentences.zh: 周成王時，封文、武先師鬻熊之曾孫熊繹於荊蠻，為楚子，居丹陽。

translations.literal: Literal: In King Cheng of Zhou’s reign, he enfeoffed Xiong Yi, great-grandson of Yuxiong, ancient teacher of Kings Wen and Wu, among the Jing-Man peoples as Viscount of Chu, residing at Danyang.
translations.idiomatic: In King Cheng of Zhou’s reign, he enfeoffed Xiong Yi, great-grandson of Yuxiong, ancient teacher of Kings Wen and Wu, among the Jing-Man peoples as Viscount of Chu, residing at Danyang.

sentences.zh: 後十餘世至熊達，是為武王，簧以彊大。

translations.literal: Literal: More than ten generations later came Xiong Da, known as King Wu, under whom Chu grew powerful.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten generations later came Xiong Da, known as King Wu, under whom Chu grew powerful.

sentences.zh: 後五世至嚴王，總帥諸侯，觀兵周室，并吞江、漢之間，內滅陳、魯之國。

translations.literal: Literal: Five generations later came King Zhuang, who led the feudal lords, reviewed troops in Zhou territory, absorbed lands between the Yangtze and Han, and internally destroyed states such as Chen and Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Five generations later came King Zhuang, who led the feudal lords, reviewed troops in Zhou territory, absorbed lands between the Yangtze and Han, and internally destroyed states such as Chen and Lu.

sentences.zh: 後十餘世，頃襄王東徙于陳。

translations.literal: Literal: More than ten generations after that, King Qingxiang moved east to Chen.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten generations after that, King Qingxiang moved east to Chen.

sentences.zh: 楚有江漢川澤山林之饒；

translations.literal: Literal: Chu possessed rich rivers, marshes, mountains, and forests of the Jiang-Han basin;
translations.idiomatic: Chu possessed rich rivers, marshes, mountains, and forests of the Jiang-Han basin;.

sentences.zh: 江南地廣，或火耕水耨。

translations.literal: Literal: south of the Yangtze the land was broad, and in places people practiced slash-and-burn and water-weeding cultivation.
translations.idiomatic: South of the Yangtze the land was broad, and in places people practiced slash-and-burn and water-weeding cultivation.

sentences.zh: 民食魚稻，以漁獵山伐為業，果蓏蠃蛤，食物常足。

translations.literal: Literal: People ate fish and rice, lived by fishing, hunting, mountain cutting, and gathering fruits and shellfish, so food was usually sufficient.
translations.idiomatic: People ate fish and rice, lived by fishing, hunting, mountain cutting, and gathering fruits and shellfish, so food was usually sufficient.

sentences.zh: 故髁窳媮生，而亡積聚，飲食還給，不憂凍餓，亦亡千金之家。

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore many drifted through life idly without accumulation; they had enough for immediate eating and drinking and did not fear cold or hunger, but neither did they produce truly great fortunes.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore many drifted through life idly without accumulation; they had enough for immediate eating and drinking and did not fear cold or hunger, but neither did they produce truly great fortunes.

sentences.zh: 信巫鬼，重淫祀。

translations.literal: Literal: They trusted in spirits and shamans and placed heavy weight on lavish sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: They trusted in spirits and shamans and placed heavy weight on lavish sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 而漢中淫失枝柱，與巴蜀同俗。

translations.literal: Literal: Hanzhong, however, had become morally lax and disorderly, sharing customs with Ba and Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Hanzhong, however, had become morally lax and disorderly, sharing customs with Ba and Shu.

sentences.zh: 汝南之別，皆急疾有氣勢。

translations.literal: Literal: The Runan subregion was generally urgent and forceful in temperament.
translations.idiomatic: The Runan subregion was generally urgent and forceful in temperament.

sentences.zh: 江陵，故郢都，西通巫、巴，東有雲夢之饒，亦一都會也。

translations.literal: Literal: Jiangling, the old capital of Ying, connected westward to Wu and Ba and enjoyed the resources of Yunmeng to the east; it too was a major metropolitan center.
translations.idiomatic: Jiangling, the old capital of Ying, connected westward to Wu and Ba and enjoyed the resources of Yunmeng to the east; it too was a major metropolitan center.

sentences.zh: 吳地，斗分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Wu region corresponds to the Dipper stellar division.
translations.idiomatic: The Wu region corresponds to the Dipper stellar division.

sentences.zh: 今之會稽、九江、丹陽、豫章、廬江、廣陵、六安、臨淮郡，盡吳分也。

translations.literal: Literal: Present-day Kuaiji, Jiujiang, Danyang, Yuzhang, Lujiang, Guangling, Luan, and Linhuai all belonged to Wu’s division.
translations.idiomatic: Present-day Kuaiji, Jiujiang, Danyang, Yuzhang, Lujiang, Guangling, Luan, and Linhuai all belonged to Wu’s division.

sentences.zh: 殷道既衰，周大王亶父興廄梁之地，長子大伯，次曰仲雍，少曰公季。

translations.literal: Literal: After Shang declined, King Tai of Zhou rose in the Qiu and Liang region. His eldest son was Taibo, second Zhongyong, and youngest Jili.
translations.idiomatic: After Shang declined, King Tai of Zhou rose in the Qiu and Liang region. His eldest son was Taibo, second Zhongyong, and youngest Jili.

sentences.zh: 公季有聖子昌，大王欲傳國焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Jili had a sage son, Chang, and King Tai wished to pass the state to him.
translations.idiomatic: Jili had a sage son, Chang, and King Tai wished to pass the state to him.

sentences.zh: 大伯、仲雍辭行采藥，遂奔荊蠻。

translations.literal: Literal: Taibo and Zhongyong withdrew on the pretext of gathering medicinal herbs and fled to the Jing-Man region.
translations.idiomatic: Taibo and Zhongyong withdrew on the pretext of gathering medicinal herbs and fled to the Jing-Man region.

sentences.zh: 公季嗣位，至昌為西伯，受命而王。

translations.literal: Literal: Jili succeeded to the position; by Chang’s time he became the Western Earl and received the Mandate to be king.
translations.idiomatic: Jili succeeded to the position; by Chang’s time he became the Western Earl and received the Mandate to be king.

sentences.zh: 故孔子美而稱曰：「大伯，可謂至德也已矣！

translations.literal: Literal: Thus Confucius praised Taibo: "Taibo may truly be said to possess perfect virtue!
translations.idiomatic: Thus Confucius praised Taibo: "Taibo may truly be said to possess perfect virtue!

sentences.zh: 三以天下讓，民無得而稱焉。」

translations.literal: Literal: Three times he yielded the realm, and the people found no words adequate to praise him."
translations.idiomatic: Three times he yielded the realm, and the people found no words adequate to praise him."

sentences.zh: 謂「虞仲夷逸，隱居放言，身中清，廢中權。」

translations.literal: Literal: This is what is meant by: "Yu Zhong and Yi Yi withdrew from the world and spoke freely; personally pure, yet balanced in what they set aside."
translations.idiomatic: This is what is meant by: "Yu Zhong and Yi Yi withdrew from the world and spoke freely; personally pure, yet balanced in what they set aside."

sentences.zh: 大伯初奔荊蠻，荊蠻歸之，號曰句吳。

translations.literal: Literal: When Taibo first fled to Jing-Man, its people submitted to him and called the polity Gou-Wu.
translations.idiomatic: When Taibo first fled to Jing-Man, its people submitted to him and called the polity Gou-Wu.

sentences.zh: 大伯卒，仲雍立，至曾孫周章，而武王克殷，因而封之。

translations.literal: Literal: After Taibo died, Zhongyong ruled. By the time of great-grandson Zhouzhang, King Wu had conquered Shang and therefore enfeoffed him.
translations.idiomatic: After Taibo died, Zhongyong ruled. By the time of great-grandson Zhouzhang, King Wu had conquered Shang and therefore enfeoffed him.

sentences.zh: 又封周章弟中於河北，是為北吳，後世謂之虞，十二世為晉所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: He also enfeoffed Zhouzhang’s younger brother Zhong north of the river; this was Northern Wu, later called Yu, and it was destroyed by Jin in the twelfth generation.
translations.idiomatic: He also enfeoffed Zhouzhang’s younger brother Zhong north of the river; this was Northern Wu, later called Yu, and it was destroyed by Jin in the twelfth generation.

sentences.zh: 後二世而荊蠻之吳子壽夢盛大稱王。

translations.literal: Literal: Two generations later, among the Jing-Man branch, the Wu ruler Shoumeng rose greatly and called himself king.
translations.idiomatic: Two generations later, among the Jing-Man branch, the Wu ruler Shoumeng rose greatly and called himself king.

sentences.zh: 其少子則季札，有賢材。

translations.literal: Literal: His youngest son was Ji Zha, a man of outstanding worth.
translations.idiomatic: His youngest son was Ji Zha, a man of outstanding worth.

sentences.zh: 兄弟欲傳國，札讓而不受。

translations.literal: Literal: His brothers wished to pass the state to him, but Ji Zha declined and would not accept it.
translations.idiomatic: His brothers wished to pass the state to him, but Ji Zha declined and would not accept it.

sentences.zh: 自大伯壽夢稱王六世，闔廬舉伍子胥、孫武為將，戰勝攻取，興伯名於諸侯。

translations.literal: Literal: Six generations from Taibo to the kingship claim of Shoumeng, Helu appointed Wu Zixu and Sun Wu as generals; through victories and conquests Wu rose to hegemonic fame among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Six generations from Taibo to the kingship claim of Shoumeng, Helu appointed Wu Zixu and Sun Wu as generals; through victories and conquests Wu rose to hegemonic fame among the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 至子夫差，誅子胥，用宰嚭，為粵王句踐所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: By his son Fuchai’s reign, Wu Zixu was executed and Minister Bo Pi was employed; Wu was then destroyed by King Goujian of Yue.
translations.idiomatic: By his son Fuchai’s reign, Wu Zixu was executed and Minister Bo Pi was employed; Wu was then destroyed by King Goujian of Yue.

sentences.zh: 吳、粵之君皆好勇，故其民至今好用劍，輕死易發。

translations.literal: Literal: Rulers of both Wu and Yue favored martial valor, and to this day their people favor sword use, take death lightly, and act quickly.
translations.idiomatic: Rulers of both Wu and Yue favored martial valor, and to this day their people favor sword use, take death lightly, and act quickly.

sentences.zh: 粵既并吳，後六世為楚所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: After Yue annexed Wu, it was itself destroyed by Chu six generations later.
translations.idiomatic: After Yue annexed Wu, it was itself destroyed by Chu six generations later.

sentences.zh: 後秦又擊楚，徙壽春，至子為秦所滅。

translations.literal: Literal: Later Qin again attacked Chu and forced a move to Shouchun; eventually Chu too was destroyed by Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Later Qin again attacked Chu and forced a move to Shouchun; eventually Chu too was destroyed by Qin.

sentences.zh: 壽春、合肥受南北湖皮革、鮑、木之輸，亦一都會也。

translations.literal: Literal: Shouchun and Hefei received shipments of hides, fish products, and timber from the southern and northern lakes, and were also major commercial centers.
translations.idiomatic: Shouchun and Hefei received shipments of hides, fish products, and timber from the southern and northern lakes, and were also major commercial centers.

sentences.zh: 始楚賢臣屈原被讒放流，作《離騷》諸賦以自傷悼。

translations.literal: Literal: At first, Chu’s worthy minister Qu Yuan was slandered and exiled; he composed works such as the Li Sao to express his grief and lament.
translations.idiomatic: At first, Chu’s worthy minister Qu Yuan was slandered and exiled; he composed works such as the Li Sao to express his grief and lament.

sentences.zh: 後有宋玉、唐勒之屬慕而述之，皆以顯名。

translations.literal: Literal: Later writers such as Song Yu and Tang Le admired and continued this style, and all gained distinguished fame.
translations.idiomatic: Later writers such as Song Yu and Tang Le admired and continued this style, and all gained distinguished fame.

sentences.zh: 漢興，高祖王兄子濞於吳，招致天下之娛游子弟，枚乘、鄒陽、嚴夫子之徒興於文、景之際。

translations.literal: Literal: After Han was founded, Gaozu enfeoffed his nephew Bi in Wu, who gathered wandering talents from across the realm; figures like Mei Cheng, Zou Yang, and Yan Fuzi flourished between the Wen and Jing reigns.
translations.idiomatic: After Han was founded, Gaozu enfeoffed his nephew Bi in Wu, who gathered wandering talents from across the realm; figures like Mei Cheng, Zou Yang, and Yan Fuzi flourished between the Wen and Jing reigns.

sentences.zh: 而淮南王安亦都壽春，招賓客著書。

translations.literal: Literal: Meanwhile the Prince of Huainan, Liu An, also made Shouchun his capital and attracted guests to compile writings.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile the Prince of Huainan, Liu An, also made Shouchun his capital and attracted guests to compile writings.

sentences.zh: 而吳有嚴助、朱賈臣，貴顯漢朝，文辭並發，故世傳楚辭。

translations.literal: Literal: Wu also produced men such as Yan Zhu and Zhu Jiachen, who rose to high status at the Han court; literary expression flourished, and thus the Chu lyric tradition was widely transmitted.
translations.idiomatic: Wu also produced men such as Yan Zhu and Zhu Jiachen, who rose to high status at the Han court; literary expression flourished, and thus the Chu lyric tradition was widely transmitted.

sentences.zh: 其失巧而少信。

translations.literal: Literal: Its chief fault was cleverness without trustworthiness.
translations.idiomatic: Its chief fault was cleverness without trustworthiness.

sentences.zh: 初淮南王異國中民家有女者，以待遊士而妻之，故至今多女而少男。

translations.literal: Literal: Early on, the Prince of Huainan ordered households with daughters to reserve them as wives for itinerant men of talent; this is why even now the region is said to have many women and comparatively fewer men.
translations.idiomatic: Early on, the Prince of Huainan ordered households with daughters to reserve them as wives for itinerant men of talent; this is why even now the region is said to have many women and comparatively fewer men.

sentences.zh: 本吳粵與楚接比，數相并兼，故民俗略同。

translations.literal: Literal: Wu and Yue bordered Chu and often annexed one another, so their customs were broadly similar.
translations.idiomatic: Wu and Yue bordered Chu and often annexed one another, so their customs were broadly similar.

sentences.zh: 吳東有海鹽章山之銅，三江五湖之利，亦江東之一都會也。

translations.literal: Literal: In eastern Wu there was copper from Yanzhang Mountain near the sea, along with the profits of the Three Rivers and Five Lakes; it was one of Jiangdong’s major centers.
translations.idiomatic: In eastern Wu there was copper from Yanzhang Mountain near the sea, along with the profits of the Three Rivers and Five Lakes; it was one of Jiangdong’s major centers.

sentences.zh: 豫章出黃金，然菫菫物之所有，取之不足以更費。

translations.literal: Literal: Yuzhang produced gold, but only in limited quantity, not enough to offset extraction costs.
translations.idiomatic: Yuzhang produced gold, but only in limited quantity, not enough to offset extraction costs.

sentences.zh: 江南卑溼，丈夫多夭。

translations.literal: Literal: South of the Yangtze the land was low and damp, and many adult men died young.
translations.idiomatic: South of the Yangtze the land was low and damp, and many adult men died young.

sentences.zh: 會稽海外有東鯷人，分為二十餘國，以歲時來獻見云。

translations.literal: Literal: Beyond Kuaiji at sea were the Dongti people, divided into more than twenty states, said to come periodically to offer tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Beyond Kuaiji at sea were the Dongti people, divided into more than twenty states, said to come periodically to offer tribute.

sentences.zh: 粵地，牽牛、婺女之分野也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Yue region corresponds to the stellar fields of Oxherd and Weaving Maiden.
translations.idiomatic: The Yue region corresponds to the stellar fields of Oxherd and Weaving Maiden.

sentences.zh: 今之蒼梧、鬱林、合浦、交阯、九真、南海、日南，皆粵分也。

translations.literal: Literal: Present-day Cangwu, Yulin, Hepu, Jiaozhi, Jiuzhen, Nanhai, and Rinan all belonged to Yue’s division.
translations.idiomatic: Present-day Cangwu, Yulin, Hepu, Jiaozhi, Jiuzhen, Nanhai, and Rinan all belonged to Yue’s division.

sentences.zh: 其君禹後，帝少康之庶子云，封於會稽，文身斷髮，以避蛟龍之害。

translations.literal: Literal: Its ruling house claimed descent from Yu, through a younger son of Emperor Shaokang, enfeoffed at Kuaiji; they tattooed their bodies and cut their hair to avoid harm from river dragons.
translations.idiomatic: Its ruling house claimed descent from Yu, through a younger son of Emperor Shaokang, enfeoffed at Kuaiji; they tattooed their bodies and cut their hair to avoid harm from river dragons.

sentences.zh: 後二十世，至句踐稱王，與吳王闔廬戰，敗之雋李。

translations.literal: Literal: Twenty generations later, Goujian became king and fought Helu of Wu, defeating him at Zuili.
translations.idiomatic: Twenty generations later, Goujian became king and fought Helu of Wu, defeating him at Zuili.

sentences.zh: 夫差立，句踐乘勝復伐吳，吳大破之，棲會稽，臣服請平。

translations.literal: Literal: When Fuchai came to the throne, Goujian attacked again while victorious; Wu then crushed Yue, forcing it to retreat to Kuaiji, submit, and sue for peace.
translations.idiomatic: When Fuchai came to the throne, Goujian attacked again while victorious; Wu then crushed Yue, forcing it to retreat to Kuaiji, submit, and sue for peace.

sentences.zh: 後用范蠡、大夫種計，遂伐滅吳，兼并其地。

translations.literal: Literal: Later, using the strategies of Fan Li and Minister Zhong, Yue attacked and destroyed Wu and annexed its lands.
translations.idiomatic: Later, using the strategies of Fan Li and Minister Zhong, Yue attacked and destroyed Wu and annexed its lands.

sentences.zh: 度淮與齊、晉諸侯會，致貢於周。

translations.literal: Literal: Crossing the Huai, Yue met with Qi and Jin leaders and presented tribute to Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Crossing the Huai, Yue met with Qi and Jin leaders and presented tribute to Zhou.

sentences.zh: 周元王使使賜命為伯，諸侯畢賀。

translations.literal: Literal: King Yuan of Zhou sent an envoy to confer the title of hegemon, and the feudal lords all offered congratulations.
translations.idiomatic: King Yuan of Zhou sent an envoy to confer the title of hegemon, and the feudal lords all offered congratulations.

sentences.zh: 後五世為楚所滅，子孫分散，君服於楚。

translations.literal: Literal: Five generations later Yue was destroyed by Chu; its descendants dispersed, and its ruler submitted to Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Five generations later Yue was destroyed by Chu; its descendants dispersed, and its ruler submitted to Chu.

sentences.zh: 後十世，至閩君搖，佐諸侯平秦。

translations.literal: Literal: Ten generations later, Min ruler Yao assisted the feudal powers in pacifying Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Ten generations later, Min ruler Yao assisted the feudal powers in pacifying Qin.

sentences.zh: 漢興，復立搖為越王。

translations.literal: Literal: After Han arose, Yao was re-established as King of Yue.
translations.idiomatic: After Han arose, Yao was re-established as King of Yue.

sentences.zh: 是時，秦南海尉趙佗亦自王，傳國至武帝時，盡滅以為郡云。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time Zhao Tuo, commandant of Nanhai under Qin, had also declared himself king; his line lasted until Emperor Wu, when it was fully abolished and converted to commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Zhao Tuo, commandant of Nanhai under Qin, had also declared himself king; his line lasted until Emperor Wu, when it was fully abolished and converted to commanderies.

sentences.zh: 處近海，多犀、象、毒冒、珠璣、銀、銅、果、布之湊，中國往商賈者多取富焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Being near the sea, the region was rich in rhinoceros horn, ivory, tortoiseshell, pearls, silver, copper, fruits, and cloth; merchants from the Central States often made fortunes there.
translations.idiomatic: Being near the sea, the region was rich in rhinoceros horn, ivory, tortoiseshell, pearls, silver, copper, fruits, and cloth; merchants from the Central States often made fortunes there.

sentences.zh: 番禺，其一都會也。

translations.literal: Literal: Panyu was one of its major urban centers.
translations.idiomatic: Panyu was one of its major urban centers.

sentences.zh: 自合浦徐聞南入海，得大州，東西南北方千里，武帝元封元年略以為儋耳、珠崖郡。

translations.literal: Literal: From Hepu and Xuwen, sailing south into the sea reached a great island, a thousand li in each direction; in Yuanfeng year 1, Emperor Wu conquered it and made the Dan’er and Zhuya commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: From Hepu and Xuwen, sailing south into the sea reached a great island, a thousand li in each direction; in Yuanfeng year 1, Emperor Wu conquered it and made the Dan’er and Zhuya commanderies.

sentences.zh: 民皆服布如單被，穿中央為貫頭。

translations.literal: Literal: People there wore cloth like a single wrap, with a hole cut in the center for the head.
translations.idiomatic: People there wore cloth like a single wrap, with a hole cut in the center for the head.

sentences.zh: 男子耕農，種禾稻紵麻，女子桑蠶織績。

translations.literal: Literal: Men farmed, planting millet, rice, ramie, and hemp; women raised silkworms and wove cloth.
translations.idiomatic: Men farmed, planting millet, rice, ramie, and hemp; women raised silkworms and wove cloth.

sentences.zh: 亡馬與虎，民有五畜，山多麈嗷。

translations.literal: Literal: There were no horses or tigers; people kept the five domestic animals, and the mountains had many deer and apes.
translations.idiomatic: There were no horses or tigers; people kept the five domestic animals, and the mountains had many deer and apes.

sentences.zh: 兵則矛、盾、刀，木弓弩，竹矢，或骨為鏃。

translations.literal: Literal: Their weapons were spears, shields, and knives, with wooden bows and crossbows and bamboo arrows, sometimes tipped with bone.
translations.idiomatic: Their weapons were spears, shields, and knives, with wooden bows and crossbows and bamboo arrows, sometimes tipped with bone.

sentences.zh: 自初為郡縣，吏卒中國人多侵陵之，故率數歲壹反。

translations.literal: Literal: After these places were first organized as commanderies and counties, many Chinese officials and soldiers oppressed the locals, so rebellions broke out every few years.
translations.idiomatic: After these places were first organized as commanderies and counties, many Chinese officials and soldiers oppressed the locals, so rebellions broke out every few years.

sentences.zh: 元帝時，遂罷棄之。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Yuan’s reign, they were finally abandoned.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Yuan’s reign, they were finally abandoned.

sentences.zh: 自日南障塞、徐聞、合浦船行可五月，有都元國；

translations.literal: Literal: From the border posts at Rinan, Xuwen, and Hepu, a five-month sea voyage reached the state of Duyuan;
translations.idiomatic: From the border posts at Rinan, Xuwen, and Hepu, a five-month sea voyage reached the state of Duyuan;.

sentences.zh: 又船行可四月，有邑盧沒國；

translations.literal: Literal: another four months by sea reached Yilumo;
translations.idiomatic: Another four months by sea reached Yilumo.

sentences.zh: 又船行可二十餘日，有諶離國；

translations.literal: Literal: another twenty-odd days by sea reached Chenli;
translations.idiomatic: Another twenty-odd days by sea reached Chenli.

sentences.zh: 步行可十餘日，有夫甘都盧國。

translations.literal: Literal: and a ten-plus-day overland journey reached Fugan Dulu.
translations.idiomatic: And a ten-plus-day overland journey reached Fugan Dulu.

sentences.zh: 自夫甘都盧國船行可二月餘，有黃支國，民俗略與珠劯相類。

translations.literal: Literal: From Fugan Dulu, sailing for more than two months reached Huangzhi, whose customs were broadly similar to those of Zhuya.
translations.idiomatic: From Fugan Dulu, sailing for more than two months reached Huangzhi, whose customs were broadly similar to those of Zhuya.

sentences.zh: 其州廣大，戶口多，多異物，自武帝以來皆獻見。

translations.literal: Literal: Its territory was vast and populous and rich in unusual goods; since Emperor Wu’s time it had regularly sent tribute audiences.
translations.idiomatic: Its territory was vast and populous and rich in unusual goods; since Emperor Wu’s time it had regularly sent tribute audiences.

sentences.zh: 有譯長，屬黃門，與應募者俱入海巿明珠、璧流離、奇石異物，齎黃金雜繒而往。

translations.literal: Literal: There was an interpreter-chief under the Yellow Gate office who sailed with recruited crews to maritime markets for pearls, glass, rare stones, and exotic items, carrying gold and mixed silks as trade goods.
translations.idiomatic: There was an interpreter-chief under the Yellow Gate office who sailed with recruited crews to maritime markets for pearls, glass, rare stones, and exotic items, carrying gold and mixed silks as trade goods.

sentences.zh: 所至國皆稟食為耦，蠻夷賈船，轉送致之。

translations.literal: Literal: At each state reached, supplies were furnished in paired allotments, and barbarian merchant ships relayed transport onward.
translations.idiomatic: At each state reached, supplies were furnished in paired allotments, and barbarian merchant ships relayed transport onward.

sentences.zh: 亦利交易，剽殺人。

translations.literal: Literal: Trade was profitable, but killings and plunder also occurred.
translations.idiomatic: Trade was profitable, but killings and plunder also occurred.

sentences.zh: 又苦逢風波溺死，不者數年來還。

translations.literal: Literal: They also suffered from storms and drowning; those not lost might return only after several years.
translations.idiomatic: They also suffered from storms and drowning; those not lost might return only after several years.

sentences.zh: 大珠至圍二寸以下。

translations.literal: Literal: Large pearls could reach up to just under two cun in circumference.
translations.idiomatic: Large pearls could reach up to just under two cun in circumference.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始中，王莽輔政，欲燿威德，厚遺黃支王，令遣使獻生犀牛。

translations.literal: Literal: In Yuanshi during Emperor Ping, Wang Mang as regent sought to display imperial prestige and virtue; he sent rich gifts to the king of Huangzhi and ordered him to dispatch a live rhinoceros.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshi during Emperor Ping, Wang Mang as regent sought to display imperial prestige and virtue; he sent rich gifts to the king of Huangzhi and ordered him to dispatch a live rhinoceros.

sentences.zh: 自黃支船行可八月，到皮宗；

translations.literal: Literal: From Huangzhi, an eight-month voyage reached Pizong;
translations.idiomatic: From Huangzhi, an eight-month voyage reached Pizong;.

sentences.zh: 船行可八月，到日南、象林界云。

translations.literal: Literal: another eight months by sea reached the border of Rinan and Xianglin.
translations.idiomatic: Another eight months by sea reached the border of Rinan and Xianglin.

sentences.zh: 黃支之南，有已程不國，漢之譯使自此還矣。

translations.literal: Literal: South of Huangzhi lay Yichengbu; from there Han interpreter-envoys turned back.
translations.idiomatic: South of Huangzhi lay Yichengbu; from there Han interpreter-envoys turned back.
